+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [2 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again. The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [2 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again. The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

urn:contentItem:6JHK-SCK3-S7MT-615V-00000-00NextEra Energy May Explore Opportunities Following Dominion Energy's SCC CERC Approval Appeal in Appalachian Voices v. Virginia SCC *Updated*2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisNextEra Energy May Explore Opportunities Following Dominion Energy's SCC CERC Approval Appeal in Appalachian Voices v. Virginia SCC *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know

This matter involves the judicial review of state regulatory approval for a natural gas reliability facility currently under review pursuant to environmental justice and clean energy statutes. The legal challenge might impact how utilities substantiate project necessity amidst increasing data-center demand and decarbonization requirements within the power generation sector. Wider industry implications may involve prolonged timelines for comparable infrastructure, modifications in risk assessments for equity and emissions compliance, and adjustments in state-federal coordination regarding reliability planning. Stakeholders are advised to observe the court's evaluation of agency analysis and statutory interpretations, as the results could impact sector-wide compliance expenses and investment confidence in reliability-oriented developments. Forward-looking assessments indicate potential improvements in permitting structures to align energy security with environmental protections.

Background of the Case

The litigation titled Appalachian Voices, Chesterfield NAACP & Mothers Out Front - Appeal of Virginia SCC CERC Approval (Supreme Court of Virginia) originates from the State Corporation Commission's November 2025 issuance of a Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity for Dominion Energy's Chesterfield Energy Reliability Center, a 944-megawatt natural gas-fired peaker plant with an estimated cost of up to $1.47 billion. The facility is located at the former Chesterfield Power Station and is designed to maintain grid reliability amidst data-center demand. Subsequent to a December 2025 petition for reconsideration submitted by the plaintiffs, the SCC upheld its approval in February 2026. The plaintiffs submitted a notice of appeal to the Supreme Court of Virginia around February 24, 2026, claiming violations of the Virginia Environmental Justice Act and the Virginia Clean Economy Act concerning environmental justice effects and the necessity for reliability. This appeal serves as an initial evaluation of these statutes within utility CPCN proceedings. Specific settlement figures are not provided at this juncture; the requested remedies concentrate on the reversal or remand of the approval. Previous regulatory activities involved substantial public commentary during the SCC proceedings.

Competitive Impacts on NextEra Energy

The current appeal highlights regulatory complexities associated with natural gas infrastructure, which could impact NextEra Energy's East Coast portfolio through indirect changes in regional energy procurement and investor sentiment regarding developers with diversified renewable and reliability pipelines that are less vulnerable to single-project appellate challenges.

Possible Opportunities for NextEra Energy

Expanded participation in regional reliability and renewable energy solicitations as peer timelines undergo regulatory review.

Strengthened client relationships for alternative capacity solutions addressing supply reliability.

Increased investor interest in NextEra's established clean energy portfolio amidst sector fluctuations.

Potential co-development or advisory roles with entities pursuing resilient, low-emission energy options.

Why This Case Is Challenging for NextEra Energy

Sector-wide regulatory effects from environmental justice and clean economy precedents may increase compliance obligations and timelines for similar infrastructure proposals. Increased review of statutory compliance could raise costs for project assessments and affect investor expectations regarding the disclosure of equity and decarbonization risks in energy developments.

Key Challenges for NextEra Energy

Indirect elevation of environmental justice requirements in regulatory evaluations.

Greater analyst and investor focus on portfolio exposure to SCC appellate proceedings.

Potential rise in mitigation and review expenditures across generation initiatives.

Competitive need to differentiate through superior compliance and engagement frameworks.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Performed enhanced portfolio assessments of equity and clean energy risks in infrastructure planning.

Advanced engagement with state agencies on regulatory transparency and data standards.

Invested in improved modeling and community monitoring technologies.

Refined contractual protections in power agreements addressing regulatory contingencies.

Key Measures

NextEra Energy has incorporated these responses via dedicated cross-functional teams under executive oversight, focusing on proactive adaptation to maintain development continuity while reinforcing regulatory resilience.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Market share growth in reliability procurements translating into additional revenue estimates of up to $400 million.

Enhanced perception as a leader in compliant energy solutions during peer challenges.

Benefits from accelerated alternative project advancements in the region.

Stronger positioning in grid planning discussions.

Potential Losses

Incremental compliance and assessment costs estimated up to $150 million across relevant assets.

Temporary sector-wide impacts on deployment schedules.

Elevated financing considerations during uncertainty periods.

Resource redirection toward advocacy and review processes.

Final Thoughts

NextEra Energy navigates a balance of strategic opportunities and indirect pressures resulting from this regulatory litigation. Utilizing its clean energy expertise enables the pursuit of growth avenues in supply markets while managing spillover compliance requirements through structured risk management. This measured approach facilitates sustained competitiveness within the utilities sector over the long term.

Activities

Dominion Energy, Inc., a leading utility holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, focuses on the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas across the United States. Its core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including fossil fuels, nuclear power, and an increasing emphasis on renewable sources such as solar, wind, and hydroelectric projects. The company's fastest-growing segments include renewable energy initiatives and clean power solutions, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory environments. Additionally, Dominion Energy is expanding its natural gas infrastructure and electric grid modernization efforts to improve efficiency and reliability, positioning itself as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while serving millions of customers in regulated and competitive markets. It is the NYSE's 2nd largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Dominion Energy, Inc. (NYSE:D) is a prominent energy company, distinctively positioned with its expansive electricity provider and natural gas utility operations. Dominion Energy amends credit agreements as part of its strategic approach to financial management, demonstrated by recent credit agreement amendment efforts to enhance its credit facility structure. Dominion Energy's investor relations debt update highlights a commitment to extending maturities and corporate debt refinancing, including a notable revolving credit facility amendment and corporate finance strategy. Emphasizing clean energy investments, Dominion Energy is advancing renewable energy initiatives with wind energy projects and solar energy projects, while its energy storage solutions bolster power generation capabilities. The firm's robust distribution network and pipeline services, alongside pivotal LNG services, strengthen its market position. Dominion Energy's bonds maturity extension underscored through senior unsecured notes and the maturity schedule, exemplifies a proactive approach in managing its debt maturity extension strategy, aligning with its long-term growth outlook.

value.Title: Dominion announces plans for new 3-gigawatt gas plant in Cumberland County

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-YFS3-RVFF-M0TG-00000-00Dominion announces plans for new 3-gigawatt gas plant in Cumberland County2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisDominion announces plans for new 3-gigawatt gas plant in Cumberland County

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Shannon Heckt

May 7th, 2026 ( Virginia Mercury - Delivered by Newstex )

A digital rendering for Dominion Energy's proposed Cumberland Energy Center, a 3,000 megawatt (3 gigawatts) combined cycle natural gas plant planned to come online by 2033 or 2034. (Photo courtesy Dominion Energy)

Dominion Energy is aiming to expand their portfolio of energy sources in Virginia with a massive 3,000 megawatt (3 gigawatts) combined cycle natural gas plant in Cumberland County. The utility is now preparing to seek state and local regulators' approval of the plant, slated to be operational in less than 10 years.

The plant will produce base load energy around the clock and will be able to provide power for up to 750,000 homes. If approved, it will be, by far, the largest natural gas facility in Dominion's Virginia fleet.

The proposed facility will outsize the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project, now in its early stages of activation and slated to produce 2,600 MW of power when complete. The company said the new project is necessary to meet soaring energy needs statewide.

'PJM (the regional electric grid operator) is forecasting that our demand will grow on average by about 5% annually over the next 20 years and more than doubling by 2045,' Jeremy Slayton, a representative for Dominion, said Thursday. 'To meet this unprecedented demand growth reliably and affordably, we are going to need to generate twice as much energy to keep up with that demand into the future.'

The utility is just beginning the application process for the plant, planned to be located on 900 acres on Duncan Store Road in the northern region of Cumberland, bordering the James River.

Before the plans can move forward, the project will have to receive approval from the county through a conditional use permit, get the green light from the State Corporation Commission and secure a Department of Environmental Quality air permit.

This process is expected to take about two years with construction anticipated to start in 2029. It will afford several opportunities for the public to voice their opinions on the plant, as energy costs and reliability remain a top concern for residents.

Dominion's recent projections show that in order to meet the growing energy demands in the region, the company will have to build an estimated six natural gas plants, on top of other renewable energy sources, over the next 20 years. These estimates have met with significant public criticism and concerns about environmental impacts.

In Dominion's most recent integrated resource plan, where the company must explain to the SCC their long-range energy production roadmap, the utility estimated that solar will be its largest growth sector, representing 53% of new energy sources. Natural gas is the second largest with 25%, wind will comprise 10%, battery storage will be 6% and small nuclear reactors will be 6%.

Last month, the SCCapproved Dominion's proposal for five solar and battery storage projects and 10 power purchase agreements totaling up to a gigawatt of power.

'What matters most to our customers is that when they go home and they flip that light switch, the power comes on,' Slayton said. 'We are pursuing projects like the Cumberland Energy Center to ensure that we are providing a reliable and affordable grid and affordable and reliable energy for our customers.'

The full cost of the new plant is yet to be determined, but Dominion said it will be a 'multi-billion dollar project.' Slayton confirmed that it will cost more than the Chesterfield Energy Reliability Center (CERC), a 944 MW gas peaker plant that is priced at $1.47 billion.

The CERC project faced years of fierce community pushback due to concerns over health impacts from the particulates that would be released into the air from the plant, as well as a push for more renewable energy sources to be utilized over fossil fuels.

SCC approves Chesterfield gas plant and Dominion rate hike, creates new rate class for data centers

'SCC approves Chesterfield gas plant and Dominion rate hike, creates new rate class for data centers' - Virginia Mercury

Compared to other natural gas plants such as their facilities in Greenville and Brunswick, Dominion said, the technology underpinning the new plant has advanced.

The Cumberland plant would include two sets of two gas turbines paired with a steam turbine that captures the residual energy from burning the gas, which will also be used for power generation.

The turbines will be hydrogen-capable, should that be a useful future energy source. A hydrogen recycling plant is operational in Petersburg and the state is working to build out the industry in Southwest Virginia.

'Because the life of these assets is over 30 years, we want the units to be as capable to be able to accept alternate fuels when and if they come available,' said Brandon Martin, Dominion's director of generation development. 'In the event that the future says hydrogen is now economic or viable for our plants, we'd like them to be able to accept hydrogen.'

Dominion estimates the project will create 450 local construction and operation jobs and will generate about $500 million in local tax revenue over the life of the project.

If permits are approved, the company anticipates the plant will come online in 2033 or 2034.

Dominion is also beholden to the Virginia Clean Economy Act that mandates the retirement of carbon emission energy production by 2045, unless the SCC approves a plant to remain in service to ensure grid reliability.

'I think as the SCC signaled with the approval of CERC late last year, there is a reliability need in the future,' Slayton said.

The utility mailed letters to residents who live around the proposed site for the project with details about the plant. There will be two informational meetings in the county with Dominion representatives later this month and in June.

Once the conditional use permit for the project is submitted to the county board of supervisors, it will later be scheduled for public hearings and a vote.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [2 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again. The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

urn:contentItem:6JHN-7S43-SK5D-328F-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE MONITOR: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 06, 20262026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE MONITOR: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 06, 2026

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [2 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again. The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again. The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027. The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040. Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability. As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [2 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again. The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.

While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses. The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website. As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Form Energy 's Iron-Air Battery Expansion Poised to Disrupt Energy Grid

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-ND63-SC00-T50J-00000-00April 28, 2026: Form Energy's Iron-Air Battery Expansion Poised to Disrupt Energy Grid2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:05ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Form Energy's Iron-Air Battery Expansion Poised to Disrupt Energy Grid

Form Energy is significantly altering the landscape of long-duration energy storage with its latest iron-air battery expansion. On 29th April 2026, the company unveiled insights into how its technology reshapes grid planning and addresses challenges that traditional lithium-ion batteries cannot effectively manage.

The core of Form Energy’s strategy lies in the structural gap it addresses, particularly in maintaining grid reliability during multi-day weather events and prolonged periods of low renewable generation. While lithium-ion batteries excel at managing short-term fluctuations in energy supply and demand, they fall short in scenarios requiring extended discharge capabilities. Form Energy's iron-air batteries offer a solution with up to 100 hours of continuous discharge, making them uniquely suited for such applications.

As Form Energy scales its operations, the company has reported a remarkable 375% increase in its active development pipeline, growing from over 200 MW / 20 GWh in October 2025 to approximately 750 MW / 75 GWh by April 2026. This surge in demand is driven by strategic partnerships, including a notable agreement with Google and Xcel Energy for a 300 MW / 30 GWh system, valued at approximately $1 billion, which further emphasizes the competitive cost structure of iron-air technology.

A key advantage of Form Energy's iron-air batteries is their reliance on abundant materials—iron, water, and air—eliminating the volatility associated with critical minerals like lithium. This not only provides a stable supply chain but also positions Form Energy favorably against competitors as the market for lithium-ion batteries faces rising costs due to supply constraints. The company's manufacturing facility in West Virginia is expanding, targeting a production capacity of at least 500 MW annually by 2028 to meet this growing demand.

The implications of Form Energy's expansion extend beyond U.S. borders, with its first international project in Ireland set to address challenges specific to high wind penetration in the region. As the company continues to build on its momentum, the evolving landscape of energy storage technology highlights the importance of strategic manufacturing and the potential for iron-air batteries to thrive in a market increasingly dependent on long-duration energy solutions.

INDEX

SECTION 1 FORM ENERGY PROFILE

SECTION 2 FORM ENERGY TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 FORM ENERGY PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Form Energy is an American energy storage technology and manufacturing company that is developing and commercializing a pioneering iron-air battery capable of storing electricity for 100 hours at system costs competitive with legacy power plants. Form''s multi-day battery will reform the global electricity system to reliably run on 100% low-cost renewable energy, every day of the year.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: Skyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity and Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio with HiveTracks

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-MNJ3-RXG0-614B-00000-00Skyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity and Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio with HiveTracks2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:57ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity and Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio with HiveTracksNASHVILLE, Tenn. and CRESTON, N.C., May 5, 2026

PR NewswireInnovative initiative integrates pollinator habitat, biodiversity monitoring, and environmental data collection across solar projectsNASHVILLE, Tenn. and CRESTON, N.C., May 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ --Skyview Ventures, a U.S.-based renewable energy investor, together with its solar company, Davis Hill Development, today announces the expansion of its partnership withHiveTracks, a biodiversity intelligence platform, to scale data-driven environmental monitoring and managed pollinator programs across its solar portfolio. The program integrates apiaries, environmental monitoring, and pollinator habitats into operating and developing solar projects across multiple U.S. states.

Skyview Ventures is both an investor in HiveTracks and the company's first solar industry client. The collaboration began in 2025 with eight solar projects across New York, West Virginia, Tennessee, and Connecticut. Following the success of the initial rollout, Skyview Ventures plans to expand the program to additional sites in 2026."This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind," said Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. "By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats, and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems."Each participating solar site is managed by local beekeepers recruited and supported by HiveTracks. These apiaries serve not only for honey production but also as environmental monitoring tools, offering insight into ecosystem health and land use impact over timeHiveTracks produces environmental metrics, visualized on a per site and portfolio level, generating actionable recommendations to improve ecological conditions and biodiversity. The data collected comprise pollinator and plant diversity and abundance, and are complemented with hive-based testing for eDNA and ecotoxicology. In 2025, the honey produced at the program's apiaries was bottled and donated to food banks and given away as gifts.The program spans projects at multiple stages of development—from pre-construction through operations—allowing Skyview Ventures to evaluate environmental impact across the full lifecycle of solar project development. Following annual analysis, HiveTracks provides site-specific recommendations that allowDavis Hill Development, the project operator, to implement pollinator-friendly operations and maintenance practices, and improve habitat conditions over time."Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further," said Maria Morales Ferrebus, Solar Development Associate at Skyview Ventures. "In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk. By implementing HiveTracks' recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats."Together, these site-level insights are informing how Skyview Ventures approaches land stewardship across its portfolio. By integrating apiaries and environmental monitoring into its solar projects, the company is creating a scalable model that supports pollinator health, enables community agriculture, and generates consistent, site-specific data to guide long-term land management decisions."Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale," said Max Rünzel, CEO of HiveTracks. "Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact."The expanded program reflects a broader trend in the renewable energy industry toward multi-use land strategies, where solar development is combined with agriculture, habitat restoration, and biodiversity initiatives.About Skyview VenturesSkyview Ventures has three operating businesses: Skyview Finance Company, one of the largest merchants of Environmental Attributes; Davis Hill Development, which develops, owns, and operates solar and battery storage facilities; and Skycharger, which develops, owns, and operates electric vehicle charging infrastructure. In addition, Skyview Ventures has over 30 venture investments on their portfolio.The company owns 70 MW of solar assets across 15 states and over 600 EV charging stations. Headquartered in Nashville, TN, with additional offices in Greenwich, CT and Sacramento, CA, Skyview Ventures is a national leader in clean energy.To learn more, visitwww.skyviewventures.com.About HiveTracksHiveTracks is building the data infrastructure for biodiversity, enabling landowners, developers, and farmers to measure and improve ecological outcomes directly on-site. HiveTracks brings environmental monitoring and sustainable agriculture to the green energy sector by leveraging beehives as biosensors and empowering beekeepers as field ecologists. To learn more, visitwww.hivetracks.com.About Davis Hill DevelopmentDavis Hill Development (DHD), a subsidiary of Skyview Ventures, is a leading developer and owner of distributed solar projects with more than 180 operating assets across 15 states. Founded in 2013, DHD delivers turnkey clean energy solutions for businesses, municipalities, and landowners, with a growing pipeline of community and commercial solar projects. The company specializes in structuring projects that generate long-term value—reducing energy costs, providing lease revenue, and supporting energy equity through community solar access for low-to-moderate income (LMI) residents. Through its parent company, DHD also offers REC and carbon credit solutions to help organizations meet sustainability goals. Learn more atwww.davishilldevelopment.comMedia Contact:Alexandra Pony

value.Jurisdiction: Virginia
value.Title: Dominion Energy planning multibillion-dollar power plant in Cumberland

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-F973-SCCF-M121-00000-00Dominion Energy planning multibillion-dollar power plant in Cumberland2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisCENTRAL VIRGINIADominion Energy planning multibillion-dollar power plant in CumberlandJosh Janney

SUMMARY:

Dominion Energy Virginia announced plans Thursday to build a multibillion-dollar 3,000-megawatt combined cycle natural gas power plant in Cumberland County, part of the utility's broader effort to meet rapidly growing power demand across the state.

The proposed Cumberland Energy Center would be built on roughly 900 acres off Duncan Store Road in northern Cumberland County and include two combined-cycle natural gas units designed to be capable of using hydrogen in the future, according to the company.

Dominion spokesperson Jeremy Slayton said the utility has not finalized an exact cost estimate because major project contracts are still being negotiated, but described the proposal as a "multibillion-dollar project."

If approved by state and local regulators, Dominion plans to start construction in 2029 and have the plant begin operations between 2033 and 2034. It will generate enough electricity to power about 750,000 homes.

The announcement comes as Dominion expands generation from multiple energy sources, including solar, battery storage, offshore wind and natural gas, amid rapidly growing electricity demand.

According to Slayton, customer demand is forecast to grow by about 5% annually over the next two decades and more than double by 2045. Slayton said roughly 75% of that projected growth is attributable to data centers.

"To meet this unprecedented growth, we need to generate twice as much energy to keep up with that demand and maintain a reliable grid for our customers," he said.

Slayton said the Cumberland facility would operate as a baseload power plant providing around-the-clock electricity while "working hand in hand" with the company's renewable energy efforts.

The utility also argued the project would reduce Virginia's reliance on costly imported electricity. Slayton said purchased power costs increased nearly 30% over the past year and that it's cheaper and more cost-effective for Dominion customers if the utility has its own generation.

"The investments we are making today will ensure our customers have reliable, affordable and increasingly clean energy for decades to come," Dominion Energy Virginia President Ed Baine said in a statement. "We are generating more power from every source to serve our customers' growing needs, make Virginia more energy independent and produce more clean energy."

Dominion said the power station will consist of two natural gas, hydrogen-capable combined cycle units. Slayton explained the plant would initially operate on natural gas and that future hydrogen use would depend on whether the technology and fuel market became cost-effective.

The company said the Cumberland project would create about 450 construction and operations jobs and generate more than $500 million in local tax revenue.

The project still requires several approvals, including a conditional-use permit from the county, an air permit from the Virginia Department of Environmental Quality and a Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity from the Virginia State Corporation Commission.

Dominion expects to submit local permit applications in the coming months and plans to file with the SCC next year. Slayton said the review process would include multiple opportunities for public input.

The announcement follows several recent developments in Dominion's energy portfolio.

In April, the SCC approved five solar and battery storage projects along with 10 solar and storage power purchase agreements totaling more than 1,000 megawatts.

Meanwhile, Dominion's Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project began delivering electricity to the grid in March. The 2,600-megawatt offshore wind farm is under construction, with nine turbines installed to date, according to the company.

The post Dominion Energy planning multibillion-dollar power plant in Cumberland appeared first on Virginia Business.
