urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G2VR-00000-00NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:05ZLexisNexisSTOCKNANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY - CURRENT NEWS FROM THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY

PermID4296599588Websitehttps://www.ntu.edu.sg/Pages/home.aspxIndustryUniversityAddress50 Nanyang Avenue, Singapore 639798

ACTIVITIES:

Nanyang Technological University (NTU), founded in 1991, is a public research university in Singapore. With over 7,000 employees and 33,000 students, it generates approximately $1.5 billion in annual revenue. Renowned for engineering, business, and sciences, NTU ranks among Asias top universities, excelling in AI, robotics, and sustainability research. Its campus hosts the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine and cutting-edge facilities like the Smart Campus initiative. In 2024, NTU expanded its green energy research, focusing on hydrogen technologies. Competing with the National University of Singapore, NTUs mission is to drive innovation and global impact through interdisciplinary education and transformative research, fostering industry partnerships and societal contributions.

+ NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ NTU ALUMNUS YAP WEE KWONG ESTABLISHES FAMILY GRATITUDE BURSARY TO HELP FINANCIALLY NEEDY UNDERGRADUATES

Yap Wee Kwong, who studied Accountancy at NTU and became the first in his family to graduate in 1996, is creating a lasting legacy for future scholars through a family-backed bursary. On 11 May 2026, he reflected on his journey and on how his daughter inspired the initiative.Back in his NTU days, financial aid was scarce, with few scholarships available. His father was a bus driver and his mother a homemaker; he relied on student loans and part-time work to fund his studies. He said, "My family background wasn't that strong. What I wanted to do was to graduate so I could work and earn money."After earning a second degree in 2005, he returned to NTU in 2025 as a parent accompanying his daughter Lynnette, who studies Environmental Sciences and Data Analytics. He was struck by the campus's transformation and the broader options now available. In June 2025, he and his wife Chan Seow Wai pledged S$150,000 to establish the Yap Wee Kwong & Family Gratitude Bursary, aimed at assisting financially needy undergraduates, particularly in the Asian School of the Environment at NTU.

+ NTU PROFESSOR ZHAO YANLI NAMED AIMBE FELLOW FOR PIONEERING WORK IN MATERIALS CHEMISTRY AND BIOMEDICAL ENGINEERING

Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) announced that Professor Zhao Yanli from the School of Chemistry, Chemical Engineering and Biotechnology has been elected as a Fellow of the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering (AIMBE). The induction occurred on April 13, 2026, during the AIMBE Annual Event in Arlington, Virginia, USA.Zhao is recognized for interdisciplinary research at the intersection of materials chemistry and biomedical engineering, advancing bioimaging, therapy and nanomedicine platforms. He ranks among the top two percent of medical and biological engineers, and the College of Fellows is regarded as one of the most prestigious professional distinctions in the field. At NTU, Zhao leads a research group focused on integrating functional systems for targeted bioimaging and disease therapy. The team has been advancing therapeutic platforms that both image diseased tissue and deliver drugs precisely where needed, including the development of single-metal atom biocatalysts for antitumor immunotherapy. These biocatalysts exhibit high catalytic activity and selectivity due to well defined structures and are designed to suit the microenvironments of different diseases. The work also explores the atomic structure of the catalysts, optimizing active sites and elucidating their biocatalytic mechanisms.

+ NTU EXPANDS AI CAPABILITIES WITH TWO NEW NTU-AI4X POSTDOCTORAL FELLOWS

Nanyang Technological University (NTU) has announced the appointment of two postdoctoral fellows as part of the NTU-AI4X initiative, aiming to broaden AI-enabled research capabilities in materials science and engineering. The move underscores NTU's commitment to advancing technological education and research.The fellows will lead projects that leverage artificial intelligence to accelerate materials discovery. The announcement, dated 8th May 2026, reflects NTU's broader strategy to weave advanced technologies into its academic framework and position the university at the forefront of global materials science research.By fostering interdisciplinary collaboration between AI and materials science, the NTU-AI4X program seeks to strengthen the School of Materials Science and Engineering and build a generation of researchers equipped with modern skills. The investment mirrors a wider academic trend toward AI-driven research and signals NTU's ongoing commitment to innovation and excellence.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ NATIONAL UNIVERSITY OF SINGAPORE [3 News That Matters]

+ NUS COMPUTING LAUNCHES MAGIC LAB TO ACCELERATE ROBOTICS AI RESEARCH

On May 11, 2026, the National University of Singapore's Computing department announced the launch of the Manipulation and General Intelligence Control Lab, or MAGIC Lab, a new hub for advancing robotics AI research centered on human-centric models for manipulation. The lab aims to bridge theory and practice through work in robot learning, 3D vision, dexterous manipulation, and simulation, with the goal of real-world deployment and safe operation.MAGIC Lab will actively recruit PhD students, research assistants, and postdoctoral researchers from robotics, computer vision, and machine learning, reflecting NUS Computing's commitment to attracting leading talent. The lab will be led by Jiafei Duan, an incoming Presidential Young Professor, who stated he is excited to establish the MAGIC Lab at the National University of Singapore: "After years of research in robotics and AI, I'm thrilled to start my own group: the MAGIC Lab at the National University of Singapore." Duan previously worked with Ai2, NVIDIA, and A*STAR’s Institute for Infocomm Research, and led the development of MolmoAct, an open-source Robotics Foundation Model, underscoring his readiness to guide the lab.

+ SRMIST AND NUS LAUNCH 15-MONTH PGDM-X FOR EXECUTIVES

SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) announced the launch of the Post Graduate Diploma in Management for Executives (PGDM-X), a 15-month program for working professionals seeking to accelerate leadership without leaving their roles. The program begins on 11th May 2026 and is offered in collaboration with the National University of Singapore (NUS).The 15-month format includes six on-campus residencies at SRMIST, five inter-residency online phases, and a seven-day international immersion at the NUS campus in Singapore, combining classroom learning, case-based discussions, leadership simulations, and industry interactions.Curriculum covers Artificial Intelligence (AI) & Machine Learning for Business Analytics, Digital Transformation & India Stack, Strategic Marketing Simulation, Leadership Communication, Entrepreneurship and Startup Management, Organisational Leadership, Financial Markets, Supply Chain Management, Design Thinking and Project Management, among others. The program also provides mentorship from global professionals and includes an international immersion week in Singapore; the fee is Rs 12.8 lakh, inclusive of tuition, immersion, accommodation, academic materials and travel related to the Singapore component.

+ IPB UNIVERSITY EXPANDS GLOBAL REACH THROUGH NUS PARTNERSHIP

IPB University is intensifying its internationalization efforts through strategic partnerships, notably with the National University of Singapore. The collaboration with NUS is currently about 70 percent complete, as the Faculty of Economics and Management seeks to expand double degree programs and boost global competitiveness for its students. The university already offers 15 double degree programs for master's degrees and continues pursuing partnerships with other universities in Malaysia and Turkey. FEM has also launched international classes in 2024, now in its third cohort, drawing students from diverse backgrounds. Through the International Thematic Real Work Lecture (KKNT) with partners in Malaysia and Thailand, IPB provides overseas experiences for about 50 students each year. In addition, IPB is investing in facilities, including an Investment Gallery with the Indonesia Stock Exchange and plans for a Capital Market School to improve investment literacy among faculty and staff. Prof Irfan Syauqi Beik, Dean of FEM, said, "We want FEM to become a national and international reference in the development of economics and management, as well as to contribute to policy formulation for national development."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HONG KONG UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY [3 News That Matters]

+ HKUST BREAKS GROUND ON NEW MEDICAL SCHOOL AMID AMBITIOUS EXPANSION

HKUST marked a milestone with the groundbreaking of its new medical education and research complex during a ceremony held on 29th April 2026. The project, part of a broader push to expand healthcare education in Hong Kong, is backed by a HK$2 billion investment to fund an eight-story facility slated for completion in mid-2028, with the first cohort of medical students expected to join soon after. HKUST president Nancy Ip outlined a multidisciplinary vision for the new school, spanning artificial intelligence, data science, healthcare management, medical ethics and health policy. She emphasized the need to collaborate with established institutions in Hong Kong, including the University of Hong Kong and the Chinese University of Hong Kong, to build on existing strengths and position the city as a global hub for medical innovation. After Hong Kong approved the establishment of the city's third medical school in November 2025, HKUST also plans a new four-year second-degree medical program to meet rising demand for healthcare professionals.

+ HKUST TO ESTABLISH THIRD MEDICAL SCHOOL TO TRANSFORM HONG KONG HEALTHCARE EDUCATION

The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST) is positioned to shape the future of medical education in Hong Kong. Following a government decision last year to establish a third medical school in the city, a groundbreaking ceremony for the Medical Education and Research Complex was held on 28th April 2026.At the ceremony, Chief Executive and HKUST Chancellor John Lee stressed that the project would boost the capacity and efficiency of Hong Kong's healthcare services. The plan calls for the first cohort of 50 students to enter in the 2028/29 academic year, with matching government funds and land set aside in the Northern Metropolis for a teaching hospital that links education, research and patient care.

+ HKUST BREAKS GROUND ON MEDICAL EDUCATION COMPLEX AIMING TO ELEVATE HONG KONG HEALTHCARE

Hong Kong can expect better healthcare services as HKUST's medical education and research complex moves into construction, said Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu at the groundbreaking ceremony this morning. It marks the city's third medical school since government approval last November.Lee expressed confidence in HKUST, saying the project will attract and train top talent and contribute to Healthy China, a key goal of the Five-Year Plan. He added that the new medical school would deliver better quality, greater efficiency, and higher capacity in the city's healthcare and medical services, while strengthening local doctor training to ease manpower shortages and address the challenges of an aging population.Three working groups formed from government departments and experts will guide HKUST through curriculum planning and financing, he noted. He also said land at Ngau Tam Mei in the Northern Metropolis has been reserved for the medical school's permanent campus and an integrated hospital for medical education, research, and patient care. At the ceremony, HKUST president Nancy Ip Yuk-yu outlined the project specifics: the eight-story complex will span 6,000 square meters and is slated for completion by mid-2028, housing a clinical training center and research labs, with seven clinical professors from the US, Europe, and Australia already hired, and a dean of medicine to be named soon. Ip emphasized that HKUST will not poach from Hong Kong's two existing medical schools and highlighted potential collaboration, noting HKUST's first cohort may take electives there while its students can enroll in AI courses; discussions remain ongoing to broaden talent development.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO [3 News That Matters]

+ UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO DEBUTS FULLY AUTOMATED ROBOTICS LAB TO TRANSFORM BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH

The University of Tokyo unveiled a fully automated robotics laboratory at the Institute of Science Tokyo's Yushima campus. In a ceremony on 12th May 2026, ten robots, including the humanoid Maholo LabDroid, began operating without human staff, signaling a shift in how experiments may be conducted. The Robotics Innovation Center plans to expand its automated workforce and weave AI into nearly every research task. The current systems have dual-arm manipulators capable of dispensing precise reagent volumes, handling temperature-sensitive equipment, and enabling automatic cell cultivation, marking a major enhancement to laboratory operations. The project aims to bolster Japan's scientific standing and reduce errors associated with manual work. The Maholo robot has already seen use in Kobe for stem cell work, and researchers from that hospital have joined the university's center to deepen collaboration. Officials say the long-term goal is to grow the robot roster to roughly 2,000 by 2040, with automatons taking on a wide range of duties from hypothesis generation to experimental verification.

+ KDDI EXPANDS STARTUP ECOSYSTEM THROUGH ALUMNI VENTURES AND UTOKYO COLLABORATION

KDDI has formed partnerships with Alumni Ventures and the University of Tokyo's UTokyo Innovation Platform to bolster cross-border startup growth. The collaborations are designed to connect Japanese and international startups with funding, mentorship, and market access, marking a step forward in KDDI's aim to deepen Japan's role in the global venture ecosystem.By teaming with Alumni Ventures in the United States and UTokyo's platform, KDDI will leverage its network, customer base, and technology services to engage early-stage companies seeking expansion beyond their home markets. The move aligns with KDDI's strategy to widen its access to innovative technologies, particularly in 5G and IoT.Investors will watch how KDDI selects startups that fit its telecom and fintech focus and how resources are allocated between these new programs and existing investments. Metrics to monitor include pilots on KDDI's network, the launch of new digital services, and enterprise solutions that integrate cybersecurity and IoT.

+ UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO EXPANDS CURRICULUM WITH MANGA-INSPIRED CULTURAL EXCHANGE

The University of Tokyo is marking a milestone by integrating manga into its curriculum in collaboration with The Student Life, a partnership designed to foster understanding between students in Tokyo and those in Claremont, California. On 9th May 2026, the collaboration was officially announced, underscoring the university's commitment to engaging students through innovative and culturally rich content.The Todai Shimbun, the university's newspaper, is leading the cross-institutional effort to promote cultural exchange and student journalism across borders. The partnership will enable students from both campuses to explore diverse writing styles and cultural narratives, deepening their understanding of each other's contexts.As part of the program, students will select a manga work and craft essays that analyze its themes and cultural significance. The assignments have included works from Osamu Tezuka's classics to modern hits like 'Attack on Titan,' reflecting the university's interdisciplinary approach to linking manga with broader visual culture.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TSINGHUA UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ TSINGHUA ADVANCES MIGRASOME RESEARCH, REDEFINING INTERCELLULAR COMMUNICATION

Tsinghua University has advanced the study of migrasomes, organelles that function as long-range signaling platforms. As of 9th May 2026, the research adds fresh context to a discovery made about ten years ago, highlighting how cells share stable, precisely controlled information beyond direct contact or soluble signals. Migrasomes are typically 0.5–3 m in diameter and carry chemokines, cytokines, growth factors, and other cargo such as mRNAs and damaged mitochondria. They act both as local secretion hubs and as delivery vehicles to distant targets, even recruiting coagulation factors to support rapid clotting at injury sites. The researchers detail a three-stage formation process—nucleation, maturation, and expansion—that ensures migrasomes are produced at the right time and place, enabling high-precision information transfer. Their findings challenge classic diffusion-based signaling and point to new diagnostic and therapeutic possibilities in angiogenesis, cancer metastasis, and immune responses.

+ TSINGHUA UNVEILS AI-DRIVEN TELESCOPE TO PROBE DARK MATTER AND DARK ENERGY

Tsinghua University is launching the Multiplexed Survey Telescope (MUST), a project that blends AI with cutting-edge astronomical hardware to study dark matter and dark energy. The telescope is scheduled for completion by 30th December 2030 and aims to capture light spectra from tens of millions of galaxies, building a three-dimensional map of the cosmos.To handle the data, researchers have developed AI algorithms for high-dimensional cosmology. Trained on extensive simulated universes, these models can identify gravitational lensing and cosmic expansion signals often masked by noise, enabling faster evaluations than conventional methods.The partnership with EPFL, which will provide the MUST fiber positioner, broadens the project’s global reach. As one lead astrophysicist noted, 'MUST is not merely a telescope; it represents a highly reusable engine of discovery capable of bringing hidden physics to light.' The initiative promises to redefine astrophysical research as it progresses.

+ STARRING ENERGY CLOSES 500 MILLION RMB SERIES A+ TO SCALE FUSION TECH

Starring Energy, the fusion venture born at Tsinghua University, announced a 500 million RMB Series A+ on May 7, 2026, strengthening its position in the rapidly evolving nuclear fusion sector. The round attracted a mix of investors including Fortune Capital, Jinpu Investment, and Shanghai Shenneng Chengyi, with continued backing from Feitu Venture Capital.With this influx, total funding now exceeds 2 billion RMB and the company is valued above 1 billion USD, officially earning unicorn status. Founded in 2021 by Tsinghua alumni Chen Rui and Tan Yi, Starring Energy is pursuing controlled fusion through its compact spherical tokamak technology aimed at reshaping fusion economics.The company’s fundraising journey has included a June 2022 angel round led by Sequoia China Seed Fund and Matrix Partners China, followed by a March 2024 Pre-A, underscoring rising confidence in its approach. Investors see controlled fusion as a cornerstone of future energy, and Starring Energy’s work on high-temperature superconducting spherical tokamak systems positions it among leading private fusion companies, with device generations such as SUNIST-2 and NTST advancing toward a first engineering demonstration reactor.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNIVERSITY OF HONG KONG [3 News That Matters]

+ HKU TO LAUNCH ENTERPRISE SUSTAINABILITY INSTITUTE WITH HK$426 MILLION DONATION

The University of Hong Kong (HKU) will receive a donation exceeding HK$426 million from The Hong Kong Jockey Club to establish the HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute and to create The Hong Kong Jockey Club Endowed Professorships, dedicated on 5th May 2026.The gift aims to merge the expertise of distinguished international scholars with local needs, fostering a robust exchange of ideas that can translate into practical policy recommendations. Rimsky Yuen, Jockey Club Steward, said the endowed chairs will help convert research into actionable solutions for a more resilient and livable Hong Kong.The dedication ceremony brought together leaders from both organizations, including Prof. Xiang Zhang, President and Vice-Chancellor of HKU, and the endowed professors named so far, such as Guojun He in Economics, Jeffrey Ng in Accounting, and Uta Schonberg in Economics, among others. The HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute aims to become a premier center for ESG practices, conducting impactful research, engaging with the community through sustainability indices and advisory services, and offering educational programs to train future sustainability professionals.

+ HKJC DONATES OVER HK$426 MILLION TO HKU TO ESTABLISH GLOBAL SUSTAINABILITY RESEARCH INSTITUTE

On Wednesday, 6th May 2026, the Hong Kong Jockey Club announced it would donate more than HK$426 million to the University of Hong Kong to establish the HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute. The gift will fund the institute’s research and operations and support the creation of the Hong Kong Jockey Club Endowed Professorships to attract distinguished international scholars addressing local needs.Rimsky Yuen, Steward of the HKJC, said the institute is envisioned as a world-class hub for environmental, social and governance best practices, focusing on three core areas: research, engagement and education. The aim is to generate actionable insights with relevance for Hong Kong and the wider region.The donation reinforces the HKJC’s longstanding commitment to HKU, helping nurture leaders who can drive economic development and support national prosperity. As the institute takes shape, it will bring together scholars, practitioners and policymakers to develop practical solutions for sustainability challenges and enhance Hong Kong’s standing in ESG research and practice.

+ HKU UNVEILS ECHECKGO AI TO MODERNIZE BUILDING INSPECTIONS IN DENSE CITIES

The University of Hong Kong has unveiled eCheckGo, an artificial intelligence system designed to modernize building inspections in dense urban environments. The iLab team, led by Professors Junjie Chen and Wilson Lu from the Department of Real Estate and Construction, announced the system on 29th of April 2026, highlighting its rapid defect identification and comprehensive building assessments.As aging buildings proliferate, roughly 14,000 privately owned structures are expected to be over 50 years old by 2030. eCheckGo analyzes large image sets in seconds, claiming to be at least 100 times faster and eight times more cost effective than traditional automated solutions, enabling proactive monitoring of building conditions for public safety.The system relies on a proprietary Large Defect Model trained on extensive data. Users can capture images with a mobile app or use Google Street View imagery, making the inspection flow seamless. After upload, the AI identifies defects such as cracks and spalling and generates interactive 3D models that visualize a building's condition to aid maintenance decisions. In a city-wide test, Google Street View images of 9,172 Kowloon buildings produced a color-coded defect map in four hours, and the results were verified by professional surveyors. Professor Chen noted that eCheckGo provides an intuitive overview of building conditions, enabling quick, informed action on defects to improve urban safety.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KYOTO UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ AWA HIGH SCHOOL BREAKS 33-YEAR GAP WITH ADMISSIONS TO UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO AND KYOTO UNIVERSITY

Tateyama, Chiba — In a milestone, students from Chiba Prefectural Awa High School have passed entrance exams for the University of Tokyo and Kyoto University this spring. The 3rd of May 2026 milestone marks the first time in 33 years that Awa High has sent scholars to these two institutions. The turning point came with the Renaissance Project launched in 2023, creating a specialized advanced class aimed at preparing students for top national universities. Among the first graduates, Hyuga Shimabuku was admitted to the University of Tokyo's Faculty of Science Division II, and Hayato Takiguchi to Kyoto University's Faculty of Agriculture. These admissions are the first University of Tokyo admission in 18 years and the first Kyoto University admission in 13 years, respectively. Principal Takahide Hayakawa emphasized local talent and resilience, stating, "Regional public high schools are in a difficult situation, but they have proven that children who remain in their communities can achieve results if they work hard. We want to continue nurturing the local children in Awa." This year, more than 30 Awa High School students were accepted to national and public universities, the first time in seven years, signaling a positive trend for the school's future.

+ TUJ KYOTO MARKS ONE-YEAR MILESTONE WITH EXPANSION AND NEW PROGRAMS

TUJ Kyoto marks its first anniversary with a slate of expansions as enrollment has tripled since inception, and the campus is rolling out new academic offerings, expanding classroom space, and upgrading facilities to meet rising demand. As of 23rd April 2026, student engagement has grown, highlighted by the campus's inaugural TEDxTUJ event held earlier this month.Since establishing its second major location near the Fushimi Inari Shrine in January 2025, TUJ Kyoto has added an extra floor of classrooms and expanded its housing stock to over 300 apartments, with many within walking distance of the campus, underscoring a commitment to a supportive environment for its diverse student body.The TEDxTUJ event, themed "Shoshin ( , Beginner’s mind)", showcased TUJ Kyoto’s global community through student-led talks and sessions by staff, faculty and students that touched on ideas from the origins of life to cultural expression, reflecting the institution’s mission as a bridge between international perspectives. The university also outlined its growth roadmap, including the September 2025 launch of an Honors Program and a Master of Science in Communication Management, complemented by an Adult Study Abroad program slated to begin in May 2026.

+ AKM AND KYOTO UNIVERSITY DEMONSTRATE 2 M PCSEL OSCILLATION, MARKING A STEP IN COMPACT INFRARED LASERS

Asahi Kasei Microdevices Corp (AKM) and the Kyoto University Institute for Advanced Study have achieved laser oscillation in a 2 m-band infrared photonic crystal surface-emitting laser PCSEL. The breakthrough was announced at the 73rd Japan Society of Applied Physics Spring Meeting, held from March 15-18, 2026, and is aimed at enabling smaller, high-performance sensing systems while retaining PCSELs directional emission and narrow spectral width. PCSEL technology originated in the early 2000s at Kyoto University under Professor Susumu Noda and offers compact functionality compared with traditional lasers. The collaboration focused on optimizing the light-source structure, and the team reports successful laser oscillation in the 2 m band, highlighting the potential for mass-producible infrared lasers with high brightness.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PEKING UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ HSE UNIVERSITY AND PEKING UNIVERSITY EXPAND EDUCATIONAL TIES IN MOSCOW TALKS

In Moscow, HSE University hosted a meeting with Peking University led by President Gong Qihuang to discuss the Cross Years of Russian–Chinese Cooperation in Education for 2026-2027. On 5th May 2026, Rector Nikita Anisimov and Gong Qihuang stressed the collaboration active since 2019 and the six agreements signed last May in a ceremony attended by Russian and Chinese officials. The institutions discussed launching joint research in AI, machine learning and mathematics, while Peking University offered to support exchanges via the Sino–Russian Association of Fundamental Sciences. Anisimov noted growing interest in Chinese studies at HSE, with nearly 1,500 students enrolled in Chinese language programs. Gong Qihuang also highlighted youth engagement and proposed expanding student mobility, including double degrees and joint dissertation training. He welcomed a new unit at HSE to manage collaborations with China. The delegation will participate in International Partners Week at HSE in Saint Petersburg, from May 18 to May 22, 2026, and Anisimov was invited to visit Peking University later in the year for international forums.

+ PEKING UNIVERSITY SPURS SURGE OF HARD-TECH IPOS BY ALUMNI

Peking University is celebrating its 128th birthday, but since May 4, 2026, the campus has become a focal point for a new wave of hard-tech IPO activity led by its alumni. Several PKU graduates' ventures are lining up for listings in the capital market, underscoring the university’s growing imprint on the sector. In 2025, Minglue Technology, Novosense Microelectronics, and Jingfang Pharmaceuticals went public, and this year Luoshi Robotics and Shenyan Intelligence are pushing ahead with IPOs, expanding alumni presence in robotics and artificial intelligence across primary and secondary markets. PKU’s innovation ecosystem, built around the Yuanpei Fund, Yanyuan Venture Capital, and the Yanyuan Incubator, is creating a pipeline from laboratory breakthroughs to commercial success. Notable examples such as Galaxy Universal Robotics and Deep Potential Technology have attracted investments, with Galaxy Universal raising more than 6 billion yuan and valued at about 21 billion yuan, while Deep Potential is reshaping scientific tools through AI. The story around Weiming Lake signals a broader shift from academia to a wealth-creating innovation landscape led by PKU graduates.

+ PEKING UNIVERSITY AND LINGNAN UNIVERSITY LAUNCH LYSSA 2026 TO FOSTER STEM ENTREPRENEURSHIP

The Lingnan-Yuanpei STEM Summer Academy (LYSSA) 2026 is a joint effort by Lingnan University and Peking University to cultivate student innovation and entrepreneurship. The opening ceremony was held virtually on Apr. 21, 2026. The seven-day programme will run on Lingnan's Hong Kong campus from July 2-8 and will explore topics aligned with the current tech innovation boom, including Intelligent Device and System for Real-Time Mosquito Monitoring, Darlink—an AI-powered communication platform—and SenseShield, which focuses on accessible feminine hygiene products for the disabled. At the ceremony, Lingnan's Prof. Raymond Chan Hon-fu, Vice-President (Academics) and Provost, noted that earlier cohorts have turned ideas into award-winning business plans and expressed optimism that this year’s participants will compete for incubation funds offered by the Lingnan Foundation, acknowledging Peking University's role in hosting LYSSA last year. Prof. Li Meng, Dean of Yuanpei College at Peking University, stressed the value of the programme’s insights and networks in helping students realise their entrepreneurial visions. The collaboration aims to reinforce integrated development in education and technology. The Lingnan Entrepreneurship Initiative (LEI) director, Prof. Albert Ko Wing-yin, delivered the first training lecture on Making Impact Through Social Innovation and Humanitarian Technology and said he hopes LYSSA projects will benefit communities, reflecting Lingnan's motto of Education for Service. The partnership also seeks to promote the Study in Hong Kong brand and nurture high-calibre talent.

urn:contentItem:6JJV-M1S3-S4S2-5208-00000-00SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKSUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS

DASHBOARD: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

PermID5000317077Websitehttp://www.suffolk.com/IndustryConstruction & Engineering

ACTIVITIES:

Suffolk Construction, founded in 1982 by John Fish in Boston, Massachusetts, is a leading U.S. general contractor. With $5.5 billion in revenue and 2,500 employees, Suffolk delivers complex projects in healthcare, education, and commercial sectors. Notable works include Bostons Millennium Tower and Miamis Brickell City Centre. Employing advanced technologies like BIM and virtual design, Suffolk enhances project efficiency and safety. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE SECURES JOBS GRANT FOR MAJOR HQ EXPANSION

In a significant move to bolster local economic development, the Jefferson County Commission has approved a jobs grant for Brasfield & Gorrie, a prominent construction firm headquartered in Birmingham. On 25th March 2026, the Commission unanimously passed the resolution, which supports the company’s expansion plans that include a new office tower, a parking deck expansion, a training and event center, and a wellness center.Commission President Jimmie Stephens emphasized the importance of Brasfield & Gorrie to the region, noting that the company has been a stalwart presence in Birmingham since its inception. "They’ve chosen to remain here although they are a worldwide company," he remarked, highlighting the firm's commitment to the local economy.The expansion represents a substantial investment of $64,584,500 from Brasfield & Gorrie, with $2,884,500 allocated for land acquisition, $56,100,000 designated for construction, and $5,600,000 earmarked for equipment. This development is expected to create 80 new jobs, each offering an hourly wage of $39.51. The county’s jobs grant, capped at $240,000, is designed to incentivize this growth.In return for its investment, Jefferson County anticipates a return of $8,000 to the general fund and $1,011,773 to the education fund, illustrating the potential benefits of supporting local businesses like Brasfield & Gorrie. Stephens expressed gratitude for the confidence the company has shown in the county, vowing to maintain a strong partnership moving forward.As the economic landscape continues to evolve, the expansion of Brasfield & Gorrie underscores the importance of local support for sustainable growth. The firm’s decision to invest in Birmingham not only strengthens its operational base but also contributes positively to the broader community.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE UNVEILS $64.5 MILLION EXPANSION PLANS FOR BIRMINGHAM HQ

Brasfield & Gorrie, one of Birmingham’s largest construction firms, is planning a major expansion at its national headquarters, a move company leaders say reflects continued growth and a long-term commitment to the city. The company presented plans to the Birmingham City Council committee on 10th March 2026 for a new office building and parking expansion at its headquarters campus along 7th Avenue South.The proposal outlines a project expected to bring millions of dollars in investment and dozens of new jobs to the city. Brasfield & Gorrie plans to construct a new four-story office building totaling roughly 80,000 square feet. According to company representatives, the first floor will serve as an event and training center designed to host gatherings and professional development opportunities, featuring a large banquet room, an auditorium, and several meeting and training rooms.The remaining three floors are planned for office space that will support Brasfield & Gorrie’s expanding national operations. Company leaders say the additional space will allow them to accommodate new employees while leaving room for future growth. In addition to the new building, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to add a fourth level to its existing parking deck, creating approximately 125 additional parking spaces for employees and visitors.As part of the expansion, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to create 80 new full-time jobs headquartered in Birmingham, with an expected average salary of about $82,000. During the council committee discussion, company leaders emphasized that the new roles will help support the firm’s operations across the country while keeping its headquarters firmly rooted in Birmingham. They stated, 'It will be jobs headquartered here in Birmingham to support our field and our operations. We’re really growing, and we’re committed to staying in Birmingham.'The project represents an estimated $64.5 million investment. Under the proposed agreement, the City of Birmingham would provide financial incentives of up to $80,000 to support the project. The proposal received approval from the council committee and will now move to the full Birmingham City Council for final consideration. If approved, company leaders say construction on the expansion could be completed by late 2027.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE RECEIVES $12M TAX INCENTIVE FOR JEFFERSON COUNTY PROJECT

Birmingham-based construction firm Brasfield & Gorrie has secured a substantial economic incentive package from state officials, aimed at bolstering its $65 million project in Jefferson County. On 20th February 2026, the Alabama Department of Commerce approved nearly $12 million in tax credits for the company, which includes $9.69 million in investment tax credits and $2.24 million in jobs tax credits.The incentives are part of a strategic initiative to promote economic development in the region and are expected to play a significant role in the execution of Brasfield & Gorrie's ambitious project. This funding will not only support the immediate development but also contribute to job creation and local investment, which aligns with the state's broader economic goals.Brasfield & Gorrie, a privately held firm recognized for its extensive portfolio in construction and project management, has been a key player in Alabama's construction landscape. The approval of these tax credits underscores the state's commitment to fostering growth within its local businesses, particularly in the construction sector, which has faced challenges amid fluctuating economic conditions.The $64.6 million project will likely enhance Brasfield & Gorrie's operational capabilities and expand its service offerings, further solidifying its market position. As the company moves forward with the development, stakeholders will be keenly observing how these incentives translate into tangible benefits for both Brasfield & Gorrie and the Jefferson County community.In conclusion, the Alabama Department of Commerce's support through this tax incentive package marks a significant milestone for Brasfield & Gorrie, reinforcing its role in driving economic progress in the region. The anticipated outcomes of this project will not only reflect on the company's growth trajectory but also on the overall economic vitality of Birmingham and its surrounding areas.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ELEVATES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS TO LEAD CAPITAL MARKETS

Gilbane Development has appointed Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, a move aimed at strengthening the firm’s investment strategies. As of 28th April 2026, Vlahakis will operate from the company’s New York City office, bringing with him a wealth of experience in real estate capital markets across various asset classes, particularly in ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.In his new role at Gilbane Development, Vlahakis will be responsible for refining and executing the firm's funding strategy. Reporting directly to James Patchett, president and CEO, he will play a pivotal role in setting the capital strategy, making crucial investment decisions, and developing strong relationships with funding and equity partners. His expertise will also extend to leading transaction structuring and negotiations, positioning Gilbane to capitalize on emerging market opportunities.Before joining Gilbane Development, Vlahakis served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he was instrumental in leading equity-raising efforts for the firm's various projects and partnerships. His prior experience also includes a role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities, further underscoring his capability in navigating complex financial landscapes. Vlahakis's extensive background equips him with the insight necessary to enhance Gilbane's standing within the competitive real estate sector.Gilbane Development, known for its commitment to quality and innovation in real estate, is strategically positioning itself for growth with this significant leadership change. By appointing Vlahakis, the firm aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its capital strategies and investment portfolio, particularly in sectors that align with evolving market demands. Stakeholders and investors will be keenly observing how Vlahakis's leadership will influence Gilbane’s future endeavors.As the real estate market continues to evolve, the importance of strong leadership in capital markets becomes increasingly evident. Gilbane Development's decision to bring on Andreas Vlahakis reflects a proactive approach to securing its capital needs and driving forward its ambitious growth plans. Investors will likely be curious to see how Vlahakis's experience will translate into tangible results for the firm.

+ WKU ANNOUNCES GILBANE PARTNERSHIP, PAVING THE WAY FOR NEW DEVELOPMENTS

The Western Kentucky University (WKU) Student Government Association (SGA) has officially announced the approval of WKU's public-private partnership with Gilbane. This significant development indicates the beginning of a collaborative effort aimed at enhancing student facilities and resources. As part of the partnership, which was formally approved by WKU’s Board of Regents on 11th December 2025, Gilbane will design and develop a new residence hall, intended to replace the outdated Douglas Keen and Hugh Poland Halls.Following the recent approval from the Kentucky Legislature on 6th April 2026, WKU is poised to commence construction on the new residence halls. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed that the partnership with Gilbane is now fully authorized, stating, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." This development not only addresses housing needs but also reflects WKU's commitment to improving the overall student experience.In addition to the housing initiative, the SGA has passed several legislative measures aimed at enhancing student resources. Among these is Resolution 7-26-S, which supports the installation of a crosswalk at a crucial intersection near student housing. The resolution highlights the safety concerns of students who currently navigate busy streets to access essential facilities, advocating for collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation for this project.Further addressing financial constraints faced by students, Bill 17-26-S was also approved, establishing the Textbooks for Toppers reimbursement program. This initiative will provide $50 reimbursements for students purchasing required course materials, a move that reflects the SGA's recognition of the financial challenges many students encounter. Additionally, other bills passed will provide essential academic supplies, such as Scantrons and lab goggles, ensuring that students have access to necessary materials without added financial burden.As WKU moves forward with its partnership with Gilbane and implements these new funding initiatives, the SGA's commitment to improving the student experience is clear. The collaboration with Gilbane not only aims to enhance student housing but also signifies a broader strategy to address various student needs and concerns, fostering a supportive academic environment.

+ GILBANE LAUNCHES LEASING FOR $135M AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECT IN D.C.

WASHINGTON, D.C. — Gilbane Development has begun leasing the second of its three-phase redevelopment at 7th Street and Barnaby Road in Southeast Washington, D.C. As of 3rd April 2026, Phase II of Barnaby & 7th includes 229 units within four new residential buildings: The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis.Construction of these buildings is slated for completion by summer 2026. The four communities feature a mix of studio to three-bedroom units serving households earning 50 and 80 percent of the area median income (AMI). Notably, twenty percent of the apartments will be set aside as Permanent Supportive Housing (PSH) units, which will be managed by the district’s Department of Homeless Services.Phase I of Barnaby & 7th consisted of The Upland, which was completed in spring 2025 and includes 169 apartments, with 34 designated as PSH units. The income restrictions for this phase ranged from 30 to 50 percent of AMI. The third phase of development is expected to include approximately 100 additional rental units in one multifamily building, bringing the project to a grand total of 498 units upon completion.During the construction of Phase II, Gilbane also built a new pedestrian walkway designed to connect all six buildings of the larger development. According to the District of Columbia Housing Finance Agency, the total project cost for Phase II was approximately $135 million, underscoring Gilbane's commitment to affordable housing in the region.As the project progresses, it is anticipated to significantly contribute to the availability of affordable housing options in Washington, D.C., while also enhancing community connectivity and providing essential support services for residents in need.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.
