urn:contentItem:6JHC-MNG3-RTGT-G01T-00000-00MARS, INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKMARS, INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: MARS, INCORPORATED

PermID5040171391Websitehttp://www.mars.comIndustryPackaged Foods & Confectionery

ACTIVITIES:

Mars, Incorporated, founded in 1911, is a family-owned global confectionery and pet care company headquartered in McLean, Virginia. Employing over 140,000 people across 80 countries, it generates over $45 billion annually. Known for brands like M&Ms, Snickers, and Pedigree, Mars operates in confectionery, pet care, and food. In 2024, it acquired Kellanova for $35.9 billion, expanding its snack portfolio. Its culture, guided by Five Principles (Quality, Responsibility, Mutuality, Efficiency, Freedom), emphasizes sustainability and employee well-being. Competing with Nestl, Mars stands out for its private ownership and sustainability goals, targeting net-zero emissions by 2050. Recent initiatives include investments in plant-based foods and digital supply chain solutions.

+ MARS, INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ MARS INC. SECURES LANDMARK CLEAN ENERGY AGREEMENT IN LITHUANIA

Mars, Incorporated has secured a significant long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) for the majority output from the Skuodas Wind Farm in Lithuania. This strategic move, announced on the 28th of April, 2026, is expected to generate approximately 490 GWh of renewable electricity annually, effectively reducing carbon emissions by around 120,000 tons each year.The Skuodas Wind Farm, which has an installed capacity of 158.4 MW, aims to provide renewable energy that will cover Mars' consumption and value chain requirements. This initiative aligns with the company's Renewables Acceleration Program, which is focused on extending clean energy usage across its operations to support its net-zero emissions objectives.Kevin Rabinovitch, Global VP of Sustainability at Mars, emphasized the importance of this agreement in facilitating real-world infrastructure that contributes to climate commitments. "This agreement with European Energy helps bring new wind power online in Lithuania and strengthens our ability to extend credible renewable electricity across our value chain," he stated. The project is scheduled to go live in 2028 and will bolster the Mars pet food manufacturing facility in Lithuania, ensuring a reliable source of renewable electricity.Through this long-term financial commitment, Mars is leveraging corporate PPAs to stimulate new wind capacity development, thereby accelerating the transition to renewable energy in Europe. In collaboration with European Energy, Mars aims to enhance Lithuania's energy mix by adding domestically produced renewable capacity, as highlighted by Jens-Peter Zink, Deputy CEO of European Energy.This announcement follows other major clean energy initiatives by Mars under its Renewables Acceleration Program, which recently included a contract to launch over 100 solar projects in Poland and a significant agreement for the K lvallen Wind Farm in Sweden. By expanding its clean energy footprint, Mars projects that its efforts will result in an estimated 10% reduction in its total carbon footprint by 2030, compared to a 2015 baseline.

+ MARS INC EXPANDS RENEWABLE FOOTPRINT WITH LITHUANIAN WIND PPA

Mars Inc has taken a significant step towards sustainability by signing a wind power purchase agreement (PPA) to support the operations of its pet food factory in Lithuania. This initiative underscores the company's commitment to reducing its carbon footprint and aligning with global trends towards renewable energy. As part of this agreement, Mars aims to transition its Lithuanian operations to greener energy sources, reflecting a broader strategy to enhance its sustainability across various manufacturing facilities.The PPA was finalized on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment for Mars Inc in its ongoing efforts to incorporate renewable energy into its operations. By harnessing wind energy, the company not only aims to lower greenhouse gas emissions but also to set a benchmark for sustainable practices within the pet food industry. This move aligns with Mars's global sustainability goals, which include achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2050.In recent years, Lithuania has made substantial progress in expanding its renewable energy capacity, hitting 6 GW of combined wind and solar generation. Mars Inc's decision to engage in a wind PPA comes at a time when renewable energy investments are gaining momentum, particularly in Europe, as companies seek to bolster their sustainability credentials. The strategic partnership with local energy providers is expected to facilitate a smoother transition to renewable energy sources and enhance operational efficiencies at the factory.The Lithuanian pet food factory plays a crucial role in Mars Inc's European supply chain, producing popular brands that cater to a growing market for pet nutrition. With this wind PPA, Mars Inc not only aims to enhance the ecological efficiency of its operations but also to establish itself as a leader in responsible manufacturing practices. This commitment to sustainability is increasingly important as consumers become more environmentally conscious and demand greener products.As Mars Inc continues to integrate renewable energy into its operations, the company is well-positioned to attract investors who prioritize sustainability and corporate responsibility. The successful implementation of the wind PPA could serve as a model for other industrial operations seeking to reduce their environmental impact while maintaining competitive advantage in a rapidly evolving market.

+ MARS INC. TAKES A STAND WITH M&M'S PROTECT THE PEANUT CAMPAIGN

Mars Inc. is launching its new global campaign, Protect the Peanut, across M&M's social media channels. This initiative, which began on 7th April 2026, features a short documentary created by BBDO Chicago to address significant consumer complaints surrounding Peanut M&M's. Notably, these complaints stemmed not from any flaws in the candy itself, but from the quality of the peanuts used in production.Peanuts, being particularly susceptible to diseases and environmental challenges, have a high failure rate, with about 30% of crops not making it from pod to plate. For M&M's, the standard is even stricter, as only 1 in 100 peanuts meets their quality requirements. To tackle this issue, Mars Inc. set out on a dual mission: ensuring the future sustainability of peanuts and promoting a new variety that stays fresher for longer. After investing approximately $10 million over a decade in research, Mars collaborated with scientists from the University of Georgia to develop more resilient peanut varieties.Mars Inc.’s efforts included an additional $12 million in premiums to support farmers in transitioning to this new peanut variety. The results have been promising, with complaints regarding Peanut M&M's reportedly decreasing by around 90%. Josh Gross, Chief Creative Officer at BBDO, noted, "Sometimes the most powerful ideas start with someone noticing something small and then starting to ask the right questions." He emphasized that the campaign is about identifying those questions and addressing them meaningfully to protect a beloved product.The Protect the Peanut campaign not only emphasizes product quality but also fosters direct engagement with consumers. M&M's is reaching out to its most passionate fans, including those who had expressed concerns on platforms like Reddit. The company is offering redemption codes to allow these consumers to experience the improved product firsthand. Amanda Davies from Mars stated, "Our pursuit of the perfect peanut led us to a much bigger challenge: securing its future," highlighting the long-term commitment required to achieve meaningful progress.Davies further explained that the initiative showcases what can be accomplished through collaboration across agriculture and science. "Protect the Peanut is really a story about what partnership can unlock, and shows, at our best, what Mars can do," she stated. The campaign reflects Mars Inc.'s dedication to building resilience in its supply chain while simultaneously enhancing its brand image.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ MONDEL Z INTERNATIONAL'S 'SNACK NA SWAK' INITIATIVE AIMS TO TRANSFORM FILIPINO SNACKING

Mondel z International has launched its 'Snack na Swak' initiative, aiming to reshape snacking habits among Filipinos. As daily life becomes increasingly fast-paced and demanding, the campaign invites consumers to slow down, tune in, and make more intentional choices — transforming what is often a mindless habit into a mindful act of self-care. This initiative was officially announced on 27th April 2026.At the heart of the 'Snack na Swak' campaign is the recognition that snacking is deeply embedded in Filipino culture. Whether it’s a quick merienda between meetings or a shared moment with family, these small pauses carry significant emotional weight. By promoting mindful snacking, Mondel z International aims to position this practice not as a restriction but as an opportunity for awareness regarding portions, nutrition, and the overall snacking experience.The initiative emphasizes education and accessibility, encouraging consumers to better understand nutrition labels and manage portion sizes within a recommended 250-calorie range, as advised by local health authorities. Caitlin Nina Punzalan, Corporate and Government Affairs Lead at Mondel z International, highlighted the campaign's inspiration, noting that snacks are often linked to concerns around obesity and diabetes, and emphasized the importance of a balanced diet that includes indulgences when consumed mindfully.An interactive session held at Common Ground in Rockwell featured registered nutritionist-dietitian Jo Sebastian, who guided participants through activities like the 'Portion Puzzle.' She stressed that mindful snacking begins with understanding nutrition labels, allowing individuals to make confident choices about what they eat. This message was echoed by Arlene Reario, President of the Philippine Association of Nutrition, who pointed out the need for clearer labeling and portion-controlled options to aid in healthier decision-making.Through 'Snack na Swak,' Mondel z International not only aims to empower consumers to make informed choices but also addresses broader public health concerns. The company recognizes its role in influencing consumption habits and has introduced portion-controlled products to make healthier options more accessible. By reframing familiar snacks like Oreo and Cadbury Dairy Milk as part of a balanced lifestyle, Mondel z International is promoting a conscious approach to snacking that can be both enjoyable and health-conscious.

+ MONDELEZ INTERNATIONAL PROMOTES ANIL VISWANATHAN TO VP - MARKETING

Mondelez International has promoted Anil Viswanathan to Vice President – Marketing. On 22nd April 2026, the company announced that Viswanathan, who has been with Mondelez for nearly eight years, previously served as Senior Director of Marketing.In his new role, Viswanathan will oversee the end-to-end delivery of the entire India brand portfolio, which includes brand equity, innovation, and activation. He will also lead consumer insights and analytics while heading the consumer digital practice for the business, working closely with both India and Global Technology Leadership.Viswanathan began his career as Associate Vice President of Strategy and New Business at Cadbury India in 2008. He played a crucial role in evolving the business strategy at Cadbury Kraft India, where he served as VP Marketing – Chocolates from 2011 to 2013.Joining Mondelez International in 2014, he relocated to Zurich as Senior Platform Manager for Global Chocolates, leading chocolate innovation initiatives. His trajectory continued upward when he returned to India in 2017 as Associate Director of Marketing for AMEA, eventually becoming Senior Director of Marketing in 2020.The promotion comes at a time when Mondelez International is focusing on enhancing its market position in India, particularly in the fast-evolving consumer landscape emphasizing digital engagement and innovative marketing strategies.

+ BOFA UPGRADES MONDELEZ (MDLZ) AMID POSITIVE MANAGEMENT INSIGHTS

Mondelez International, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDLZ) has recently caught the attention of investors as BofA analyst Peter Galbo raised the firm's price recommendation on the snack giant to $65 from $62. This upgrade comes after positive feedback from management during meetings held in March, with BofA maintaining a Buy rating on Mondelez shares. The announcement has generated interest, especially given the company's inclusion among the 13 NASDAQ stocks with the highest dividends.On 13th April 2026, BofA reiterated its favorable stance on Mondelez, citing a seemingly positive update regarding ongoing negotiations with European chocolate retailers. These discussions are critical for Mondelez, as they represent potential growth opportunities in key markets. The firm has expressed confidence in Mondelez's prospects within the packaged food and protein sector, an area that is expected to witness steady demand amid evolving consumer preferences.In contrast, UBS has taken a more cautious approach, lowering its price target on Mondelez to $62 from $63 while maintaining a Neutral rating. The firm anticipates that the first quarter results for many companies in the consumer staples sector, including Mondelez, will show some improvement, with organic revenue growth stabilizing. However, UBS raises concerns about inflationary pressures that could affect earnings in the latter half of 2026, which adds an element of uncertainty to the overall market outlook.Mondelez International specializes in producing and selling a diverse range of snack products, including chocolate, biscuits, and baked snacks. Additionally, the company has interests in related categories, such as gum, candy, cheese, grocery items, and powdered beverages. This broad portfolio positions Mondelez strategically to capitalize on changing consumer habits and preferences, which are increasingly leaning towards convenience and indulgent snacking.As investors weigh their options, some analysts suggest that while Mondelez presents a compelling case for investment, certain artificial intelligence stocks may offer greater upside potential with less downside risk. Notably, there is growing interest in stocks that could benefit from geopolitical trends such as onshoring and tariff impacts. Investors are encouraged to conduct thorough research before making any investment decisions, particularly in light of evolving market conditions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER WEIGHS SEPARATION OF FOOD ASSETS AMID PORTFOLIO SHIFT

Unilever Plc is contemplating a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to enhance growth in its beauty, personal care, and wellbeing brands. This exploration comes as the Anglo-Dutch consumer goods giant evaluates its extensive portfolio. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions revealed that Unilever is engaging with advisers to assess various options, including the potential divestment of its food business, which includes well-known brands such as Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes.The deliberations are in their preliminary stages, with Unilever considering multiple pathways, including a full spin-off of its food operations or retaining select flagship brands while divesting the remainder. However, it is unlikely that any formal decision will be made before 2027. Industry insiders estimate that a transaction involving Unilever's food brands could be valued at tens of billions of dollars, although the company has yet to finalize its course of action.Unilever's food division, which includes products like Colman's condiments and the divisive Marmite spread, has seen its shares fluctuate marginally, trading 0.7% lower recently and placing the company's market capitalization at approximately £107 billion ($142 billion). Under the leadership of CEO Fernando Fernandez, Unilever is undergoing a transformative phase, having already spun off its ice cream division last year, retaining a minority stake that it intends to gradually sell.Analysts suggest that Fernandez's inclination towards beauty brands is evident, with Barclays analyst Warren Ackerman noting that while the direction is clear, the timing for a food business separation might not be optimal given the current market conditions. Unilever has previously divested other food assets, including its global spreads division and snack brands, leaving it with around €1 billion to €1.5 billion in local food brands still to be disposed of.The challenges facing major food companies like Unilever are significant, as consumers increasingly seek budget-friendly options and gravitate toward private-label products. In contrast, the beauty sector has emerged as a robust growth avenue, appealing to a diverse consumer base. As Unilever continues to refine its focus on brands such as Dove and Liquid IV, the potential separation of its food assets could reshape its future direction and investment landscape.

+ UNILEVER'S AI REVOLUTIONIZES BEAUTY & WELLBEING INNOVATION

In a significant shift for the beauty and wellbeing sector, the company is leveraging artificial intelligence to transform its approach to product development and consumer insights. On 28th April 2026, it was revealed that the firm is utilizing AI to decode consumer trends and analyze decades of research data, enabling the creation of products in mere days rather than months.As consumers increasingly seek transparency about ingredients and efficacy, the need for rapid innovation has never been more critical. The company's €12.8 billion Beauty & Wellbeing division is using AI and machine learning to assess consumer insights 60% faster than traditional methods, allowing teams to analyze brand sentiment and engagement from over 1,000 external data sources monthly. This real-time intelligence informs the development of products that resonate with evolving consumer preferences.Central to this transformation is the deployment of unique AI insights tools that synthesize findings with extensive research and development databases. By streamlining the formulation process, the company has reduced the number of cycles from five or six rounds to just one or two, while the time taken to develop concepts has been slashed from months to days. The claims generation process has also become 75% quicker, significantly enhancing the overall efficiency of product development.In addition to speeding up formulation cycles, the company is tapping into its extensive R&D datasets to explore biological data, thereby conducting complex research at unprecedented speeds. The introduction of virtual cohorts allows scientists to simulate how various demographics will respond to new products, drastically cutting both time and costs associated with early-stage testing without forgoing real-world trials.AI-driven innovations have already led to successful product launches, such as the Hydra Miracle range, which features a groundbreaking ingredient that enhances skin hydration. Furthermore, the company's Damage Therapy range is experiencing double-digit growth, showcasing how AI is not just a tool for efficiency but a catalyst for creativity and scientific discovery. As the Chief R&D Officer noted, structured data combined with human creativity is redefining the possibilities in research and development, paving the way for a new era of innovation.

+ UNILEVER'S POTENTIAL $33 BILLION FOOD ARM SALE TO MCCORMICK SPARKS INVESTOR INTEREST

Unilever Plc is reportedly in discussions to sell its food division to McCormick & Co., a deal that could reshape the landscape of both companies. The proposed transaction, valued at approximately €29 billion ($33 billion), marks the most significant change for Unilever since its inception nearly a century ago. The announcement was made public on 26th April 2026, with Unilever confirming it has received an offer from the Maryland-based spices and seasonings giant, although the outcome of the negotiations remains uncertain.Should the sale proceed, it would represent the largest acquisition in McCormick's history, given its current market capitalization of $14.5 billion. Unilever, with a valuation of £101 billion ($135 billion), dwarfs McCormick, making the endeavor particularly ambitious. While specifics regarding financing have not been disclosed, industry insiders suggest that the deal could be structured as a Reverse Morris Trust, a financial maneuver designed to be tax-efficient for both companies involved.The divestiture of Unilever's food unit would signify a strategic shift away from competing with major food conglomerates such as Kraft Heinz Co., Nestl SA, and PepsiCo Inc. This move could transform Unilever into a powerhouse focused predominantly on household and personal care products, aligning it more closely with brands like L’Or al SA and Est e Lauder Cos. Analysts have speculated that a separation of all or part of Unilever's food business has been on the table for some time, with the company actively exploring options to streamline its operations.As consumer preferences evolve and the market landscape shifts, Unilever's potential offload of its food arm reflects broader trends in the industry, where companies are increasingly focusing on their core competencies. This potential sale could also allow Unilever to allocate resources more effectively toward its personal care and beauty divisions, which have shown robust growth. The unfolding situation has piqued investor interest, as the outcome may significantly impact both Unilever and McCormick's future strategies.Investors and market watchers will be closely monitoring the developments in this high-stakes negotiation, as a successful transaction could not only redefine Unilever's business model but also elevate McCormick's presence in the global market. With both companies at pivotal points in their trajectories, the implications of this potential deal could resonate throughout the consumer goods sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GENERAL MILLS [3 News That Matters]

+ GENERAL MILLS EXECUTES €1.7 BILLION NOTES OFFERING AMID CAPITAL STRATEGY

General Mills, Inc. has announced a significant financial maneuver with the agreement to sell €1.7 billion in aggregate principal amount of junior subordinated notes. The offering consists of €1 billion of 4.750% Series A fixed-to-fixed reset rate notes and €700 million of 5.250% Series B fixed-to-fixed reset rate notes, both due in 2056. This strategic move is part of the company's broader financial strategy to optimize its capital structure. The announcement was made on 17th April 2026.The transaction was formalized under an Underwriting Agreement dated 9th April 2026, involving prominent financial institutions such as Barclays Bank PLC, Deutsche Bank AG, London Branch, Citigroup Global Markets Limited, and J.P. Morgan Securities plc. The issuance of these notes will be governed by an Indenture dated 1st February 1996, as amended, with U.S. Bank Trust Company, National Association serving as Trustee. This collaboration with leading financial entities highlights General Mills' proactive approach in managing its capital needs.The sale of these notes has been registered under the Securities Act of 1933, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards. The closing of this transaction is anticipated to occur on 16th April 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. This move underscores General Mills' commitment to maintaining a robust financial position and enhancing shareholder value in a competitive market.Investors may find this offering intriguing as it not only reflects General Mills' strategic financial planning but also signals the company's confidence in its long-term growth prospects. With the proceeds from the notes, General Mills aims to further strengthen its balance sheet and create additional value for its stakeholders.For those considering an investment in General Mills, Inc., it is crucial to analyze the company's profitability and stock valuations closely. Understanding the potential warning signs and market dynamics will be essential for making informed investment decisions in the wake of this substantial notes offering.

+ COULD GENERAL MILLS' OMNICHANNEL STRATEGY PROPEL FUTURE GROWTH?

General Mills, Inc. is increasingly positioning omnichannel execution as a central pillar of its 'Remarkable Experience' framework, underscoring its role in driving more consistent consumer engagement across both digital and physical retail environments. On the 14th of April 2026, the company demonstrated that its strategic focus on online and physical retail integration is delivering tangible results.A primary driver of this success has been General Mills' ability to utilize its extensive portfolio scale and high-impact marketing events to achieve differential retail execution. The company has ramped up investments in retail media to better capture consumers who prioritize convenience alongside value. This targeted approach to winning online has yielded significant dividends, with General Mills recently reporting a tripling of its e-commerce growth across its top five e-commerce retail partners.This omnichannel momentum is a critical leading indicator for General Mills' ability to drive profitable growth. By partnering closely with major retailers, the company is not only attracting new consumers—household penetration increasing in seven of the top ten North America retail categories through the fiscal third quarter—but also strengthening its competitive positioning in digital spaces. The integration of its online and offline strategies appears to be a well-calibrated response to shifting consumer preferences.Investors are keenly watching how General Mills leverages its e-commerce capabilities to sustain this growth trajectory. The company's enhanced focus on omnichannel retailing could potentially serve as a catalyst for future performance, especially as it continues to adapt to the evolving market landscape. With a robust strategy in place, General Mills is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for convenience-oriented shopping experiences.As the retail environment continues to evolve, General Mills' commitment to an omnichannel approach may not only fortify its market presence but also provide a blueprint for success in a competitive industry. Investors will be monitoring upcoming earnings reports closely to gauge the effectiveness of this strategy and its impact on the company's long-term growth prospects.

+ GENERAL MILLS MOVES TO STRENGTHEN CAPITAL STRUCTURE WITH €1.7 BILLION NOTE OFFERING

General Mills Inc. (NYSE:GIS) has announced the issuance of €1.7 billion in junior subordinated notes, designed to bolster its capital structure and provide financial flexibility. The offering, made public on 14th April 2026, includes €1 billion of 4.750% Series A notes and €700 million of 5.250% Series B notes, both of which feature fixed-to-fixed reset rate structures.The Series A notes will maintain a fixed interest rate of 4.750% until 16th July 2031, after which the rate will reset based on the Five-Year Swap Rate plus an initial margin of 202.4 basis points. The Series B notes will carry a 5.250% rate until 16th July 2034, with subsequent resets tied to the same swap rate plus an initial margin of 239.0 basis points. This structured approach aims to provide a predictable income stream for investors while offering General Mills a flexible financing option.Both series of notes come with step-up provisions that add 25 basis points to the margin beginning five years after their respective first reset dates, and an additional 75 basis points starting 20 years after the first reset dates. The interest payments for these notes are scheduled to occur annually on 16th July, commencing in 2026, which aligns with the company's cash flow management strategy.General Mills retains the right to defer interest payments for up to 10 consecutive years during any deferral period, with any deferred payments accruing additional interest at the applicable rate. This feature provides the company with a buffer in case of unforeseen financial pressures, allowing it to maintain operational stability.Additionally, the company has the option to redeem the notes at any time under the terms outlined in the prospectus supplement. The classification of these notes as junior subordinated debt indicates a lower claim on assets in the event of liquidation, but it also suggests a strategic move by General Mills to leverage its capital structure for future growth initiatives.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HERSHEY COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ HERSHEY COMPANY REDEFINES SUSTAINABILITY STRATEGY FOR MODERN CHALLENGES

The Hershey Company is taking a significant step towards redefining its sustainability strategy, moving away from broad, often unrealistic commitments towards a more pragmatic approach. On 27th April 2026, Hershey unveiled its new ‘Source. Make. Delight.’ framework, aiming to embed sustainability into its core operations rather than treating it as an ancillary task. This shift comes as the company faces increasing pressures from climate change, supply chain challenges, and evolving consumer expectations, which demand more accountability and transparency in corporate practices.In a recent episode of FoodNavigator-USA’s Soup-To-Nuts Podcast, Whitney Mayer, Hershey’s Head of Global Sustainability, discussed the motivations behind this strategy. Mayer emphasized that previous sustainability goals, often set for 2040 or 2050, lacked the operational focus needed for immediate impact. By shifting the conversation from lofty ambitions to actionable practices, Hershey aims to better align its sustainability initiatives with business performance, creating a more resilient and efficient operation.The ‘Source. Make. Delight.’ strategy is structured around three key pillars. The first pillar, ‘source,’ focuses on building resilient supply chains that enhance the ecosystem in which Hershey’s farmers operate. Mayer noted the importance of sustainability in cocoa sourcing, the company’s most critical ingredient. This approach not only addresses environmental concerns but also ensures long-term viability for farmers, allowing them to plan sustainably rather than react to immediate pressures.The second pillar, ‘make,’ revolves around operational efficiency, emphasizing waste reduction and energy efficiency throughout Hershey’s manufacturing and logistics processes. Mayer highlighted that reducing resource consumption not only benefits the environment but also enhances the company’s bottom line. This proactive stance is intended to future-proof the business against market volatility and evolving consumer demands.Lastly, the ‘delight’ pillar seeks to connect sustainability initiatives directly with consumer experiences. Mayer pointed out that modern consumers expect responsible business practices, and Hershey aims to meet this expectation through innovative products and transparent storytelling. The incorporation of data-driven decision-making within this strategy is essential for tracking progress and maintaining credibility amidst rising scrutiny over corporate sustainability claims.

+ HERSHEY INDIA LAUNCHES CRUNCHY COOKIE CHOCOLATE SPREAD TO CAPTIVATE YOUNG CONSUMERS

Hershey India Pvt Ltd., a subsidiary of The Hershey Company, has launched its latest product, the 'Crunchy Cookie' chocolate spread, as part of its strategy to expand its breakfast offerings. This launch, dated 24th April 2026, aims to cater to the growing demand for indulgent breakfast options among younger consumers.The new 'Crunchy Cookie' chocolate spread combines the rich flavor of creamy chocolate with the delightful texture of crunchy cookies, targeting Millennials and Gen Z consumers who seek innovative and sensory experiences during breakfast. Hershey India's marketing campaign, featuring the hashtag #TwoGoodTogether, highlights the product's appeal through relatable storytelling.Hershey India has positioned itself as a brand that promotes togetherness, aligning with Indian cultural values that emphasize family and shared experiences during meals. By expanding its portfolio to include the 'Crunchy Cookie' chocolate spread, Hershey India aims to enhance its existing offerings, which include variants of its popular chocolate spreads.Ankit Desai, Marketing Director at Hershey India, stated, 'As a leading premium chocolate brand, we are committed to meeting the evolving needs of our consumers with innovative products. The launch of the 'Crunchy Cookie' chocolate spread reflects our focus on delivering unique and enjoyable breakfast options for our customers.'With a strong presence in the Indian market, Hershey India continues to explore new product developments that resonate with consumer preferences, ultimately driving growth in the highly competitive food industry.

+ HERSHEY'S CONNECTED WORKER INITIATIVE: A NEW ERA IN MANUFACTURING

At The Hershey Company, we’ve laid a strong foundation for our digital transformation in manufacturing. On the 20th of April 2026, Hershey launched its Connected Worker program, which aims to empower factory teams with mobile tools for real-time insights, accelerated training, and improved communications throughout the manufacturing process.The Connected Worker initiative is designed to bring factories up to speed with modern technology, as many are lagging behind consumer electronics by about 10 to 15 years. In every plant where Hershey has introduced this program, there has been palpable excitement and energy among employees, signaling a significant shift in workplace dynamics. This program is expected to enhance productivity and generate substantial savings by advancing the company's digital and technological capabilities.The Connected Worker program replaces traditional paper checklists and manuals with intuitive digital tools. Previously, factory operators relied heavily on printed materials, leading to inefficient communications and inconsistent training. With this new approach, operators are digitally connected via mobile devices, giving them real-time access to production data, training resources, and each other. This ensures that knowledge is consistently disseminated across shifts, roles, and plants, allowing for an efficient workflow.Beyond enhancing connectivity, the Connected Worker technology has further implications for training and knowledge retention. By leveraging generative AI, Hershey can create instructional materials that are easily accessible to workers. For instance, a QR code on equipment can instantly provide relevant training materials. This digitization facilitates easier information retrieval and aids in faster onboarding and continuous learning for employees.The impact of the Connected Worker program extends beyond operators; it benefits maintenance teams and supervisors as well. Maintenance requests are tracked transparently, allowing teams to prioritize effectively, while supervisors gain real-time visibility into performance metrics. This streamlined data flow enables leaders to dedicate more time to coaching and supporting their teams, fostering a more engaged workforce. Hershey aims to implement this program across all manufacturing facilities, including salty snacks and confection factories, within the next 18 months, marking a significant milestone in its journey toward enhanced operational efficiency.

The J.M. Smucker Company is navigating a complex landscape as it balances strong coffee performance against challenges in its Sweet Baked Snacks segment. During Smucker's fiscal Q3 2026 earnings call, held on 13th April 2026, management highlighted the resilience of its coffee business, while also acknowledging ongoing struggles in the snacks category, exacerbated by a plant fire-related disruption.The juxtaposition of robust coffee profits against the backdrop of weakened Sweet Baked Snacks illuminates how segment dynamics and operational challenges are reshaping Smucker's earnings risk profile. Investors are increasingly focusing on whether the company's coffee-driven strength can offset the volatility seen in its snack offerings. Smucker hinges on the ability of its coffee segment to deliver consistent profits while navigating the complexities of its snack business. + J.M. SMUCKER REVAMPS FRUIT SPREAD PACKAGING AFTER 30 YEARS

The J.M. Smucker Company has unveiled a fresh packaging design for its Smucker’s fruit spreads, marking the first visual overhaul in nearly three decades. The new jars began hitting shelves this spring, starting from 6th April 2026, and they build on the brand’s iconic ’90s-era design with bolder colors, larger fruit imagery, and an expanded use of its signature gingham pattern.The gingham, which has long been a staple on the jar lid, now extends to the front label as well. Each flavor will feature a distinct color aimed at improving on-shelf identification, while the recipe and ingredients remain unchanged. "This redesign was about honoring the Smucker’s brand’s most iconic assets and evolving them with modern intention," said Dayna Lewallen, senior design manager, creative and design at The J.M. Smucker Company.The packaging refresh coincides with a growing trend of ’90s nostalgia in food and consumer culture. Smucker’s is embracing this sentiment while modernizing its shelf presence to compete in a segment where visual differentiation is key to driving trial among consumers. The company is also repositioning the updated packaging to suggest expanded usage occasions, including breakfast, snacking, charcuterie boards, and yogurt bowls, thereby moving beyond its traditional association with morning meals.For retailers, the transition to the new packaging will occur over the coming months without any concerns regarding reformulation or ingredient management, as the product inside the jar will remain unchanged. The visual enhancements are intended to facilitate quicker flavor identification on store shelves and provide the Smucker’s brand with a more contemporary presence in the spreads aisle.Based in Orrville, Ohio, J.M. Smucker leads several major grocery categories, including coffee, peanut butter, and fruit spreads. The company’s diverse brand portfolio includes well-known names such as Folgers, Dunkin’, Caf Bustelo, Jif, Uncrustables, Hostess, Milk-Bone, and Meow Mix. As Smucker’s embarks on this packaging update, it aims to strengthen its market position and appeal to a broader audience of consumers.

+ FERRERO INTERNATIONAL [3 News That Matters]

+ TOM BRADY PARTNERS WITH FERRERO FOR $100 MILLION WORLD CUP CAMPAIGN

In a significant move in sports marketing, former NFL quarterback Tom Brady has secured a partnership with Ferrero North America for the brand’s upcoming World Cup campaign. As announced on 30th March 2026, this collaboration positions the seven-time Super Bowl champion at the center of Ferrero’s ambitious ‘Go All In’ promotion, which marks the company’s largest-ever marketing commitment at $100 million.The campaign is designed to resonate with a North American audience as the FIFA World Cup approaches the region. Sports marketing agency Ten Toes played a crucial role in this partnership, identifying Brady as the ideal talent for the initiative, facilitating the collaboration, and overseeing the ongoing relationship. The partnership underscores Ferrero's strategy to bridge the gap between soccer's global appeal and its cultural presence in the United States.A commercial featuring Tom Brady is set to launch across various streaming platforms, including Netflix, Amazon, and HBO, as well as on social media channels. Michael Johnson, Managing Partner at Ten Toes, emphasized the unique opportunity this partnership presents. He stated, “While the World Cup represents the pinnacle of global sport, soccer has historically struggled to command the same cultural weight in the United States as it does in the rest of the world. By involving one of the most decorated American sports personalities of his generation, we created an opportunity to bridge the gap between a global sporting moment and a domestic audience.”Ferrero's investment in this campaign is indicative of a broader trend where brands are increasingly looking to leverage high-profile athletes to connect with consumers. With Tom Brady's extensive fan base and his ability to transcend sports, the collaboration is expected to enhance Ferrero's visibility and engagement during the World Cup period.As the tournament draws closer, all eyes will be on how effectively Ferrero can utilize Tom Brady's star power to captivate audiences and drive brand awareness. The partnership not only reflects a strategic marketing maneuver by Ferrero but also showcases the growing intersection of sports and consumer brand engagement in North America.

+ FERRERO PARTNERS WITH TOM BRADY FOR $1 MILLION SOCCER PROMOTION

Ferrero International is set to sweeten the soccer viewing experience this summer through a high-profile partnership with NFL legend Tom Brady. Starting on April 1, fans can partake in Ferrero's 'Go All In' promotion, which offers a chance to win a staggering $1 million alongside numerous daily and weekly prizes. This initiative coincides with the upcoming soccer tournament, a global event that captures the hearts of millions, and is designed to engage consumers while enhancing brand visibility.The promotional campaign, which runs from 1st April 2026 to 31st July 2026, invites customers to buy any two Ferrero products to enter the contest. Alternatively, fans can enter for free, ensuring accessibility for a broad audience. The prizes include a grand prize of $1 million, a Super Fan trip to one of the competing nations, and various branded merchandise such as jerseys and gym bags, appealing to soccer enthusiasts across the United States.Tom Brady’s involvement adds a unique flair to Ferrero's 'Go All In' promotion. Known for his competitive spirit, Brady expressed his enthusiasm for the partnership, emphasizing the shared values of quality and bringing people together. As part of the campaign, a dedicated 30-second advertisement featuring Brady will air on various platforms including Tubi, Netflix, and YouTube, beginning on April 1. The commercial aims to rally fans around soccer while promoting Ferrero's diverse product lineup.Ferrero’s comprehensive approach includes a substantial investment of $100 million, marking the company's largest marketing commitment to date. This portfolio-wide campaign encompasses not only traditional advertising but also digital activations, influencer collaborations, and eye-catching in-store displays. As the soccer tournament approaches, Ferrero is keen to leverage its extensive portfolio of beloved brands such as Nutella, Ferrero Rocher, and Butterfinger to drive consumer engagement.Michael Lindsey, President and Chief Business Officer of Ferrero North America, highlighted the strategic importance of the global soccer tournament as a cultural touchstone. By crafting high-quality products that enhance shared experiences, Ferrero aims to transform casual consumers into avid fans of its entire product range. This initiative not only seeks to elevate Ferrero’s brand visibility during a peak season for soccer but also underscores the company’s commitment to innovation and community engagement across North America.

+ BARRY CALLEBAUT AND FERRERO JOIN FORCES IN UK COCOA COALITION

Major chocolate manufacturers Barry Callebaut, Ferrero, The Hershey Company, and Tony’s Chocolonely have announced their membership in the UK Cocoa Coalition, a significant collective effort aimed at enhancing sustainability in cocoa supply chains. This coalition, which includes various NGOs and retailers, is calling for the swift implementation of the UK Forest Risk Commodities Regulation, as part of a broader initiative to eliminate deforestation linked to cocoa sourcing.The coalition's formation comes in response to growing concerns over environmental degradation and human rights violations within the cocoa industry. The announcement, made on 26th March 2026, underlines the commitment of these industry giants, including Ferrero, to not only improve transparency in their supply chains but also to support farmers' livelihoods. Richard Laming, head of public affairs at Ferrero UK, emphasized the necessity of collaborative efforts, stating that “one single entity cannot address the challenges the cocoa industry is facing alone.”By aligning themselves with NGOs such as the Rainforest Alliance and the Fairtrade Foundation, as well as retailers like Sainsbury’s and Marks & Spencer, Barry Callebaut and Ferrero are signaling a shift in industry standards. The coalition aims to establish a baseline for sustainability rather than treating it as a competitive differentiator. This collective action is expected to reshape global cocoa sourcing practices, particularly as the UK is recognized as the world's third-largest market for chocolate confectionery.Belinda Borck, public affairs lead at Tony’s Chocolonely, echoed Laming's sentiments, advocating for a shared responsibility among companies in addressing illegal deforestation and human rights issues in the cocoa sector. The coalition's unified stance calls for effective legislation that can drive systemic change, underscoring that sustainability efforts cannot be accomplished in isolation. This cooperative approach is anticipated to foster a more resilient cocoa industry, benefiting both farmers and the environment.In conclusion, the establishment of the UK Cocoa Coalition, with major players like Ferrero and Barry Callebaut at the forefront, marks a pivotal moment for the chocolate industry. By pushing for stronger regulatory frameworks and shared standards, the coalition is laying the groundwork for ethical cocoa production to become the norm. As the industry progresses towards these goals, the future of cocoa-based chocolate appears promising, with a focus on sustainability that could ensure the longevity of this cherished commodity.

PepsiCo, Inc. is actively pursuing productivity initiatives to shield its performance from the adverse effects of rising inflation. These initiatives not only bolster margin resilience but also offer flexibility for reinvestment in growth areas, including marketing, innovation, and value creation, ultimately reinforcing PepsiCo's competitive stance.In addition to its productivity measures, PepsiCo is benefiting from a diversified portfolio and strong global demand, which further support its ability to navigate inflationary pressures. The company's focus on driving volume growth, expanding in international markets, and capitalizing on emerging segments like energy drinks and functional beverages enhances its resilience. + PEPSICO PARTNERS WITH STATKRAFT IN LONG-TERM RENEWABLE ENERGY INITIATIVE

PepsiCo, Inc. has signed a 10-year virtual power purchase agreement (VPPA) with clean energy producer Statkraft, aimed at decarbonizing its operations and supply chain in Europe. This agreement, announced on 4th May 2026, represents a significant step in the beverage giant's sustainability journey.The renewable energy generated under this decade-long agreement will come from a wind project in Spain, which is currently undergoing modernization to enhance its electricity output. PepsiCo anticipates that the clean electricity produced will lead to a reduction of approximately 32,000 metric tons of carbon dioxide emissions annually, as outlined in a release dated April 28.As the lead buyer, PepsiCo has collaborated with two key suppliers—ingredients manufacturer Givaudan and sustainable packaging company Smurfit Westrock—to secure advantageous commercial terms and access long-term renewable energy opportunities typically reserved for larger buyers. This collaboration is part of PepsiCo's broader 'pep+ Renew' program, launched with Schneider Electric in 2022, which aims to assist supply chain partners in transitioning to renewable electricity.PepsiCo has engaged Schneider Electric’s SE Advisory Services to structure this significant VPPA with Statkraft. This marks the second cohort of the pep+ Renew initiative to sign a VPPA and is notable as the first European renewable electricity cohort. Archana Jagannathan, PepsiCo’s chief sustainability officer for Europe, Middle East, and Africa, emphasized the agreement's role in fostering access to renewable energy solutions and supporting the transition to cleaner power.The VPPA aligns with the goals of PepsiCo's broader sustainability framework, the PepsiCo Positive (pep+) program, which was initially launched in 2021. As part of its sustainability strategy reset, PepsiCo has adjusted its net-zero target date from 2040 to 2050, using 2022 as its baseline emissions year. This recalibration comes after the company indicated that it would likely not meet its 2025 sustainability targets, prompting new packaging sustainability goals and updated emissions reduction targets for 2030.

+ PEPSICO PARTNERS WITH SUPPLIERS FOR RENEWABLE ENERGY IN SPAIN

PURCHASE, N.Y. - PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) has taken a significant step in its sustainability journey by signing a 10-year Virtual Power Purchase Agreement (VPPA) with suppliers Givaudan, Smurfit WestRock, and Statkraft for a repowered wind asset in Spain. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, further solidifying PepsiCo's commitment to renewable energy as part of its broader pep+ REnew program.The deal is designed to aggregate renewable electricity demand, with PepsiCo acting as the lead buyer, thereby enhancing the sustainability credentials of all parties involved. The renewable electricity generated through this initiative is projected to reduce CO emissions by approximately 32,000 metric tons annually. The wind asset will be upgraded with more efficient turbines while leveraging existing infrastructure like substations and interconnection points.This partnership marks the second cohort completed under the pep+ REnew program, and notably, it is the first renewable electricity cohort established in Europe. Since its launch in 2022, the program has expanded to support over 250 companies across multiple global regions, including North America, Latin America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. PepsiCo has set ambitious climate goals, targeting a 42% reduction in Scope 3 Energy & Industry emissions and a 30% reduction in Scope 3 Forest, Land & Agriculture emissions by 2030, based on a 2022 baseline.Archana Jagannathan, Chief Sustainability Officer for PepsiCo Europe, Middle East, and Africa, emphasized the importance of the agreement, stating, "This agreement with Statkraft is a further step forward in our journey to reduce emissions not only within our own operations but across our entire value chain." This initiative follows another power purchase agreement initiated in Spain in 2023, as PepsiCo continues to prioritize sustainability in its operations.On the financial front, PepsiCo reported nearly $94 billion in net revenue in 2025 and is currently trading at $154.10 with a P/E ratio of 24.2, suggesting the stock may be undervalued. The company has a strong track record, having raised its dividend for 53 consecutive years, with a yield of 3.69%. Recent analyses from various financial institutions indicate continued investor confidence, with UBS maintaining a Buy rating and a price target of $186, reflecting optimism about PepsiCo's growth prospects.
