urn:contentItem:6JK7-JTR3-RSBB-S0MT-00000-00NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKNANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY

PermID4296599588Websitehttps://www.ntu.edu.sg/Pages/home.aspxIndustryUniversityAddress50 Nanyang Avenue, Singapore 639798

ACTIVITIES:

Nanyang Technological University (NTU), founded in 1991, is a public research university in Singapore. With over 7,000 employees and 33,000 students, it generates approximately $1.5 billion in annual revenue. Renowned for engineering, business, and sciences, NTU ranks among Asias top universities, excelling in AI, robotics, and sustainability research. Its campus hosts the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine and cutting-edge facilities like the Smart Campus initiative. In 2024, NTU expanded its green energy research, focusing on hydrogen technologies. Competing with the National University of Singapore, NTUs mission is to drive innovation and global impact through interdisciplinary education and transformative research, fostering industry partnerships and societal contributions.

+ NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ FROM LOANS TO LEADERSHIP: YAP WEE KWONG FUNDS NTU BURSARY FOR FUTURE SCHOLARS

NTU alumnus Yap Wee Kwong, who studied Accountancy and became the first in his family to graduate in 1996, has established the Yap Wee Kwong & Family Gratitude Bursary after being inspired by his daughter. On 11 May 2026, he reflected on his journey and the goal of expanding access for undergraduates.During his NTU days, financial aid was scarce; he relied on student loans and part-time work. He said, "My family background wasn't that strong. What I wanted to do was to graduate so I could work and earn money."In 2025, after earning a second degree in 2005, he returned to NTU as a parent with his daughter Lynnette, who studies Environmental Sciences and Data Analytics. He noted campus changes and the broader opportunities now available, including the option to pursue second majors or double degrees.In June 2025, he and his wife pledged S$150,000 to establish the Yap Wee Kwong & Family Gratitude Bursary, aimed at financially needy undergraduates, especially those in the Asian School of the Environment. The fund seeks to support capable students facing financial obstacles and to spur a cycle of giving, he said: "If we can contribute, we should try to do so as much as we can."

+ NTU HONORS ZHAO YANLI WITH AIMBE FELLOWSHIP FOR BREAKTHROUGHS IN TARGETED IMAGING AND THERAPY

Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) announced that Professor Zhao Yanli from the School of Chemistry, Chemical Engineering and Biotechnology has been elected as a Fellow of the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering (AIMBE). His induction occurred on April 13, 2026, at the AIMBE Annual Event in Arlington, Virginia, USA.The distinction recognizes Zhao’s pioneering interdisciplinary research in materials chemistry and biomedical engineering, with contributions spanning bioimaging, therapy and nanomedicine platforms. He is ranked among the top two percent of medical and biological engineers, and the AIMBE College of Fellows is among the sector’s highest professional honors.At NTU Zhao leads a group that builds integrated functional systems for targeted bioimaging and disease therapy. The team has advanced therapeutic platforms that enable optical imaging while delivering drugs precisely where needed, including work on single-metal atom biocatalysts for antitumor immunotherapy.These biocatalysts combine high activity and selectivity with well defined structures and are designed to suit the microenvironments of different diseases. Ongoing studies examine the atomic structure of the catalysts, optimizing active sites and clarifying the mechanisms behind their biocatalytic actions.

+ TWO POSTDOCS JOIN NTU-AI4X TO ACCELERATE AI-DRIVEN MATERIALS DISCOVERY

NTU has welcomed two postdoctoral fellows to the NTU-AI4X initiative to strengthen AI-enabled research in materials science and engineering.The fellows will lead projects that apply AI to accelerate discovery of new materials, with the announcement dated 8th May 2026.The program aims to deepen interdisciplinary collaboration between artificial intelligence and materials science and to bolster NTU's School of Materials Science and Engineering's leadership, reflecting a broader move by academia toward AI-integrated research.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ NATIONAL UNIVERSITY OF SINGAPORE [3 News That Matters]

+ MAGIC LAB AT NUS SETS SIGHTS ON PRACTICAL, EMBODIED ROBOTICS AI

NUS Computing announced the formation of MAGIC Lab on May 11, 2026, a specialized lab dedicated to advancing robotics AI through human-centric manipulation models. The research agenda centers on robot learning, 3D vision, dexterous manipulation, and simulation with the aim of real-world deployment and reliable operation.Led by an incoming Presidential Young Professor, Jiafei Duan, the lab will actively recruit PhD students, research assistants, and postdoctoral researchers from robotics, computer vision, and machine learning to build collaborative, interdisciplinary teams. The move reflects a broader industry trend toward embodied AI that combines model reasoning with sensors, hardware constraints, and deployment realities.

+ SRMIST-NUS COLLABORATE ON ACCELERATED 15-MONTH PGDM-X FOR EXECUTIVES

SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) and the National University of Singapore (NUS) are launching the 15-month PGDM-X for executives, commencing 11th May 2026.The program combines six on-campus residencies at SRMIST, five online inter-residency phases and a seven-day international immersion at the NUS campus in Singapore.The curriculum includes modules such as Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning for Business Analytics, Digital Transformation and India Stack, Strategic Marketing Simulation, Leadership Communication, Entrepreneurship and Startup Management, Organisational Leadership, Financial Markets, Supply Chain Management, Design Thinking and Project Management, with mentorship from global professionals.The fee is Rs 12.8 lakh and covers tuition, the Singapore immersion, accommodation, materials and travel.

+ NUS TIE-UP ELEVATES IPB UNIVERSITY'S DOUBLE-DEGREE AMBITIONS

IPB University is expanding its global footprint through a growing set of international partnerships, including a collaboration with the National University of Singapore that aims to deepen double degree offerings. The FEM is tracking about 70 percent completion on the NUS arrangement as it aligns curricula to boost student mobility and competitiveness, while IPB continues to solicit partnerships with campuses in Malaysia and Turkey. Currently, IPB offers 15 master's double degree programs and is pursuing further alliances to broaden international exposure. Since launching international classes in 2024, the program has reached its third cohort, attracting students from diverse backgrounds such as Afghanistan. The KKNT initiative, run with partners in Malaysia and Thailand, offers overseas study experiences to roughly 50 students each year. To support quality improvements, IPB is building facilities such as an Investment Gallery with the Indonesia Stock Exchange and planning a Capital Market School for faculty and staff to improve investment literacy. The institution has seen rising research output, with average publications per faculty member increasing from 0.4 to 0.8 over the past two years. Prof Irfan Syauqi Beik, Dean of FEM, emphasizes the goal of making FEM a national and international reference in economics and management and contributing to policy development at the national level.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HONG KONG UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY [3 News That Matters]

+ HKUST EXPANDS HEALTH TALENT PIPELINE WITH NEW MEDICAL SCHOOL

The groundbreaking ceremony for HKUST's new medical education and research complex took place on 29th April 2026, signaling a major expansion in HKUST’s healthcare offerings. The project has a budget of HK$2 billion for an eight-story building scheduled to be completed in mid-2028, with the first class of medical students to be admitted shortly thereafter. The event also underscored government support under the 15th Five-Year Plan and the aim to raise Hong Kong’s profile in medical talent. President Nancy Ip highlighted the school’s breadth, including AI, data science, healthcare management, ethics and health policy, and the intention to collaborate with HKU and CUHK. She added that HKUST will launch a four-year second-degree medical program after the November 2025 approval to establish the city’s third medical school, addressing rising demand.

+ HKUST'S THIRD MEDICAL SCHOOL TO ALIGN WITH NATIONAL PLANS AND BUILD A TEACHING HOSPITAL

HKUST is moving to establish Hong Kong's third medical school to shape the future of healthcare education, with a groundbreaking ceremony held on 28th April 2026.The university plans for a first cohort of about 50 students in the 2028/29 academic year and for government support to fund the teaching hospital in the Northern Metropolis as part of a broader effort to integrate education, research and patient care.President Nancy Ip underscored HKUST's commitment to technology and interdisciplinary collaboration, noting that the curriculum will draw on artificial intelligence, data science, medical management and ethics to nurture clinically competent and humanistic doctors.The project aligns with national policy goals, and the institution expects to recruit multiple clinicians as potential faculty while maintaining collaboration with existing Hong Kong medical schools rather than poaching staff.

+ GROUNDBREAKING FOR HKUST MEDICAL EDUCATION HUB PROMISES ENHANCED TRAINING AND COLLABORATION

Hong Kong is set to see enhanced healthcare as HKUST advances the construction of its medical education and research complex, a move announced by Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu at the groundbreaking ceremony this morning. The project is the city's third medical school since approval last November.Lee emphasized that HKUST's initiative will attract and train leading medical talent and contribute to Healthy China, a central objective of the Five-Year Plan. He highlighted anticipated gains in care quality, efficiency, and capacity, along with stronger local doctor training to relieve manpower shortages and meet the demands of an aging population.Three working groups comprising government officials and experts will steer curriculum planning and financing for HKUST, he noted. Land at Ngau Tam Mei in the Northern Metropolis has also been reserved for the school’s permanent campus and an integrated hospital for education, research, and patient care.At the ceremony, Nancy Ip Yuk-yu of HKUST described the eight-story complex as spanning 6,000 square meters and slated for completion by mid-2028. The facility will host a clinical training center and research labs, with seven international clinical professors already on board and a dean of medicine expected to be named soon. Ip stressed that HKUST will not poach staff from Hong Kong’s existing medical schools and pointed to potential collaboration with those institutions, including electives for HKUST students and AI courses for their students; discussions on broader partnerships are ongoing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO [3 News That Matters]

+ UNIVERSITY OF TOKYO SETS BOLD 2040 TARGET: 2,000-ROBOT LAB TO LEAD GLOBAL RESEARCH

The University of Tokyo unveiled a new autonomous robotics lab at the Institute of Science Tokyo's Yushima campus, with ten robots including Maholo LabDroid, operating without human staff since the 12th May 2026 opening. The Robotics Innovation Center plans significant expansion of its robotic workforce and aims to weave AI into almost every research function; current units perform precise reagent transfers, manage temperature-sensitive equipment, and support automatic cell cultivation. The initiative targets Japan's global scientific standing; the Maholo robot already works in Kobe on iPSC-related tasks, and Kobe researchers have joined the Tokyo center. The project envisions a total of about 2,000 robots by 2040 to handle much of the research process from hypothesis through verification.

+ KDDI FORMS CROSS-BORDER STARTUP PARTNERSHIPS WITH ALUMNI VENTURES AND UTOKYO

KDDI has formed partnerships with Alumni Ventures and UTokyo Innovation Platform to support cross-border startup growth, connecting Japanese and international startups with funding, mentoring, and market access.The move leverages both U.S. and Japanese channels to broaden KDDI's venture pipeline and gain exposure to technologies that could complement 5G and IoT offerings, while supporting fintech ambitions.Investors will be watching how startups are selected and how capital is allocated, with milestones including pilots on KDDI's network, new digital services, and enterprise solutions that integrate cybersecurity and IoT.

+ TODAI ADDS MANGA ANALYSIS TO CURRICULUM TO DEEPEN GLOBAL PERSPECTIVES

The University of Tokyo is expanding its curriculum by incorporating manga through a collaboration with The Student Life, to deepen understanding between Tokyo and Claremont students. The partnership was announced on 9th May 2026, highlighting the university's push for innovative, globally minded education.The Todai Shimbun, the university’s newspaper, is spearheading the cross-border cultural exchange and student journalism aspect of the program, enabling learners from both campuses to explore diverse narratives and perspectives.A core element is the manga seminar led by Associate Professor Kentaro Miwa of the Graduate School of Arts and Sciences. Since 2024, Miwa has introduced first-year students to academic analysis of manga, guiding them through works from classic to contemporary and fostering critical thinking.Participants will select a manga and write essays on its themes and cultural significance, with references to Osamu Tezuka's classics and titles such as 'Attack on Titan,' underscoring the interdisciplinary approach that connects manga to broader visual culture.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TSINGHUA UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ TSINGHUA RESEARCHERS OUTLINE THREE-STAGE MIGRASOME FORMATION AND ITS IMPLICATIONS

Tsinghua University is expanding the frontiers of migrasome research, focusing on how these organelles facilitate cell-to-cell signaling. As of 9th May 2026, the work synthesizes decades of inquiry into a framework for long-range information transfer. Migrasomes are 0.5–3 m in diameter and carry a variety of signaling molecules and cargo, including mRNAs and damaged mitochondria, enabling both local and distant communication. The formation occurs in three stages—nucleation, maturation, expansion—ensuring timely production at the right cellular locales. This mechanistic clarity helps reinterpret how cells coordinate in complex environments. These findings carry implications for angiogenesis, coagulation, tumor metastasis, and immune responses, and they point toward new diagnostic and therapeutic approaches arising from migrasome biology.

+ TSINGHUA'S MUST AIMS TO DECODE DARK MATTER WITH ADVANCED AI AND INTERNATIONAL PARTNERSHIP

Tsinghua University is pioneering the Multiplexed Survey Telescope (MUST) to probe dark matter and dark energy. Scheduled for completion by 30th December 2030, MUST blends advanced AI with modern astronomical hardware to build a three-dimensional map of the cosmos.To manage the data, researchers are developing AI algorithms for high-dimensional cosmology. Foundation models trained on simulated universes help identify gravitational lensing signals and cosmic expansion patterns that are often masked by noise, enabling more efficient analysis than traditional pipelines.With EPFL supplying the MUST fiber positioner, the project expands its reach in global astrophysics. The combination of precision optics and AI software promises to illuminate dark matter haloes and measure dark energy's repulsive force. As one lead astrophysicist at Tsinghua noted, 'MUST is not merely a telescope; it represents a highly reusable engine of discovery capable of bringing hidden physics to light.'

+ STARRING ENERGY REACHES UNICORN STATUS AFTER 500 MILLION RMB A+ FUNDING

Starring Energy, a fusion venture from Tsinghua University, raised 500 million RMB in a Series A+ on May 7, 2026, backed by Fortune Capital, Jinpu Investment, and Shanghai Shenneng Chengyi, with Feitu Venture Capital continuing support.The new funding lifts total capital to over 2 billion RMB and pushes the valuation above 1 billion USD, granting unicorn status.Founded in 2021 by Chen Rui and Tan Yi, the company focuses on compact spherical tokamak technology to advance fusion economics.Its fundraising track includes a June 2022 angel round from Sequoia China Seed Fund and Matrix Partners China, and a March 2024 Pre-A, reflecting steady confidence as it moves toward commercialization with SUNIST-2 and NTST.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNIVERSITY OF HONG KONG [3 News That Matters]

+ HKU AND JOCKEY CLUB FORM ESG RESEARCH CENTER THROUGH MAJOR GIFT

A donation totaling over HK$426 million from the Hong Kong Jockey Club will fund the HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute and endow several professorships at The University of Hong Kong, with a formal dedication on 5th May 2026.The project seeks to merge international scholarship with local needs to generate policy-ready insights, enabling research to inform decision makers. Rimsky Yuen, Jockey Club Steward, said the endowed chairs will help transform research into actionable solutions for a more resilient Hong Kong.The dedication ceremony included attendees from HKU and the Jockey Club, including Xiang Zhang, President and Vice-Chancellor of HKU, and several appointed professors such as Guojun He in Economics, Jeffrey Ng in Accounting, and Uta Schonberg in Economics.The institute aims to be a leading ESG hub, emphasizing rigorous research, community engagement through sustainability indices and advisory services, and educational programs to train the next generation of sustainability professionals.

+ HKJC ENDOWED PROFESSORSHIPS TO ANCHOR HKU SUSTAINABILITY INSTITUTE

On Wednesday, 6th May 2026, the HKJC announced a donation of over HK$426 million to the University of Hong Kong to establish the HKU Jockey Club Enterprise Sustainability Global Research Institute. The funds will support the institute and finance the Hong Kong Jockey Club Endowed Professorships, aimed at attracting distinguished international scholars to address local priorities.The initiative was celebrated at a dedication ceremony, reinforcing the commitment to turning research into policy guidance and practical solutions tailored to Hong Kong’s needs. The plan positions the institute as a hub for ESG excellence, with emphasis on research, engagement and education.Rimsky Yuen, Steward of the HKJC, noted the club’s long history of supporting HKU and said the collaboration would strengthen Hong Kong’s role in sustainability research and its application across Asia.

+ HKU DEBUTS ECHECKGO AI WITH RAPID DEFECT DETECTION FOR CITY BUILDINGS

The University of Hong Kong has launched eCheckGo, an artificial intelligence system designed to transform building inspections in dense urban environments. The iLab team, led by Professors Junjie Chen and Wilson Lu from the Department of Real Estate and Construction, announced the project on 29th of April 2026, highlighting rapid defect identification and comprehensive assessments.As aging buildings rise in Hong Kong, around 14,000 privately owned structures are expected to be over 50 years old by 2030. The system claims to scan thousands of images in seconds, delivering performance at least 100 times faster and eight times cheaper than conventional automated solutions, enabling proactive monitoring for public safety.eCheckGo runs on a proprietary Large Defect Model trained on extensive data sets to detect defects across building types. Users can capture images with a mobile app or opt to use Google Street View imagery, enabling a smooth inspection flow. When images are uploaded, the AI identifies defects such as cracks and spalling and generates interactive 3D models that illustrate a building’s condition for maintenance planning.In a city-wide test using Google Street View imagery of Kowloon, 9,172 buildings were processed to produce a color-coded defect map in four hours, with results validated by professional surveyors. Professor Chen stressed that eCheckGo provides an intuitive overview of building conditions, allowing quick action on defects and potentially improving urban safety in high-density settings. He noted ongoing discussions with government and industry about broader adoption and future enhancements, including water leakage detection and automated reporting.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KYOTO UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ RENAISSANCE INITIATIVE SPURS AWA HIGH SCHOOL SUCCESS WITH UT AND KYOTO UNIVERSITY ADMISSIONS

The admissions are notable milestones: UT has welcomed its first Awa High School entrant in 18 years, and Kyoto University its first in 13 years. The program's success underscores the potential of focused local education and diligent students, even in regions without major cram schools nearby. Principal Takahide Hayakawa emphasized local talent, saying, "Regional public high schools are in a difficult situation, but they have proven that children who remain in their communities can achieve results if they work hard. We want to continue nurturing the local children in Awa." This year, more than 30 Awa High School students were accepted to national and public universities, the best figure in seven years.

+ TUJ KYOTO REPORTS RAPID GROWTH, ADDS HOUSING AND ACADEMIC TRACKS

TUJ Kyoto is celebrating its first anniversary with rapid growth, tripling enrollment and rolling out new programs, while expanding classrooms and upgrading facilities to meet demand. By 23rd April 2026, student engagement has risen, punctuated by the campus’s first TEDxTUJ event held earlier this month.Since establishing its second site near the Fushimi Inari Shrine in January 2025, the campus added an extra floor of classrooms and boosted housing to over 300 units, many within walking distance, reinforcing support for a diverse student body.The TEDxTUJ event, themed "Shoshin ( , Beginner’s mind)", demonstrated the campus community through student-led talks and contributions from staff, faculty, and students on topics from the origins of life to cultural expression. Plans for the near term include an Honors Program and a Master of Science in Communication Management arriving in September 2025, with an Adult Study Abroad program launched in May 2026 to broaden access to Japanese language and culture.

+ KYOTO UNIVERSITY, AKM REPORT 2 M PCSEL OSCILLATION AT JSAP SPRING MEETING

Asahi Kasei Microdevices Corp (AKM) and Kyoto University's Institute for Advanced Study announced a laser oscillation in a 2 m-band PCSEL, demonstrated at the 73rd JSAP Spring Meeting on March 15-18, 2026. The PCSEL concept, developed in the early 2000s by Kyoto University researchers led by Susumu Noda, aims to deliver high-performance lasers in a compact form. The collaboration optimized the light-source structure to achieve 2 m-band operation, signaling a path toward small, mass-producible infrared lasers with tight spectral characteristics. The 2 m band supports sensitive gas detection for greenhouse gases such as CO2 and CH4, and leveraging PCSEL properties could enhance precision in trace-gas measurements for environmental monitoring and healthcare applications. AKM plans to advance manufacturability and photonic-crystal designs to capitalize on this technology.

+ PEKING UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ HSE AND PEKING UNIVERSITY ALIGN ON JOINT RESEARCH AND STUDENT MOBILITY

In Moscow, a meeting between HSE University and Peking University, led by Gong Qihuang, focused on expanding cooperation under Cross Years 2026-2027. On 5th May 2026, Anisimov and Gong Qihuang highlighted a collaboration active since 2019 and six agreements signed in May 2025 with high-level participation. The discussions covered joint research in AI, ML and mathematics, with Peking University offering to facilitate exchanges through the Sino–Russian Association of Fundamental Sciences, and noted growing Chinese-language enrollments at HSE (nearly 1,500 students). Gong Qihuang spoke about expanding student mobility, double degrees and joint dissertation training, and welcomed a dedicated unit at HSE to oversee China projects. The Peking University delegation will participate in International Partners Week at HSE from May 18-22, 2026, and Anisimov was invited to visit Peking University later in the year.

+ PKU ALUMNI LEAD CHARGE IN CHINA'S HARD-TECH UNICORN RISE

Peking University is celebrating its 128th birthday, but since May 4, 2026, its alumni are shaping a new hard-tech IPO wave. Last year 2025 saw Minglue Technology, Novosense Microelectronics, and Jingfang Pharmaceuticals go public; this year Luoshi Robotics and Shenyan Intelligence are pushing IPOs forward. The PKU ecosystem, via the Yuanpei Fund, Yanyuan Venture Capital, and the Yanyuan Incubator, has built a pipeline from lab to market, supporting startups and alumni. Prominent cases like Galaxy Universal Robotics and Deep Potential Technology illustrate impact: Galaxy Universal has raised over 6 billion yuan and is valued near 21 billion yuan, while Deep Potential uses AI to redefine scientific tooling. The Weiming Lake narrative signals a broader shift toward wealth creation through alumni-led innovation.

+ HK-TIERED LYSSA 2026 TO PROPEL STUDENT INNOVATION THROUGH CROSS-UNIVERSITY PARTNERSHIP

Lingnan University and Peking University have joined forces to run the LYSSA 2026 STEM Summer Academy, a seven-day programme designed to spark innovation and entrepreneurship. The opening ceremony was held virtually on Apr. 21, 2026. The programme will take place on Lingnan's Hong Kong campus from July 2-8 and will address themes tied to the tech-upsurge, including Intelligent Device and System for Real-Time Mosquito Monitoring, Darlink, and SenseShield. The initiative aims to connect education with advanced technology, and participants may access incubation funds from the Lingnan Foundation. Peking University supported LYSSA last year, highlighting the continuing collaboration between the two institutions. At the event, Prof. Raymond Chan Hon-fu of Lingnan highlighted past cohorts’ success in turning ideas into award-winning plans, while Prof. Li Meng of Peking University stressed the importance of the networks formed during the programme for aspiring entrepreneurs. The first training lecture, led by LEI’s Prof. Albert Ko Wing-yin on Making Impact Through Social Innovation and Humanitarian Technology, emphasized community benefits, aligning with Lingnan's Education for Service ethos.

value.Title: Smithfield Foods Releases 24Th Annual Sustainability Report

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-N523-S7BT-M4S7-00000-00Smithfield Foods Releases 24Th Annual Sustainability Report2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:06ZLexisNexisSmithfield Foods Releases 24Th Annual Sustainability Report

SMITHFIELD, Va., May 12, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Smithfield Foods released its 24 th annual sustainability repor highlighting the company's continued progress in animal care, environmental stewardship, food safety and community support. “Our team delivered meaningful progress against our sustainability targets in 2025, reinforcing our commitment to producing 'Good food. Responsibly.®,'” said Shane Smith, president and chief executive officer of Smithfield Foods.“From reducing food waste to investing in animal care and environmental stewardship, we are proud of the impact our teams are making across our operations and local communities.”

Key Achievements in 2025
Smithfield's 2025 sustainability report outlines several milestones demonstrating the company's continued focus on producing Good Food, doing Good Work and being Good Stewards:


Food safety: Maintained zero product recalls across Smithfield's U.S. operations in 2023, 2024 and 2025.

Workplace Health and Safety: Continued to achieve safety incident rates below industry averages in its U.S. operations.

Food loss and waste: Achieved a 57% reduction in food loss and waste in its U.S. manufacturing facilities, exceeding its 2030 goal five years ahead of schedule.

Renewable energy: Continued progress in producing clean, low-carbon renewable energy from on-farm methane capture through its biogas joint ventures.

Animal welfare: Achieved compliance with ISO Technical Specification 34700, an internationally recognized standard for animal welfare management.

Sustainable packaging: Reduced virgin petroleum-based plastic use by more than 50% compared to its 2019 baseline and achieved 85% of materials in recycle-ready, reusable or compostable packaging.

Supply chain efficiency: Optimized transportation routes using GIS technology, eliminating more than 1 million miles in East Coast feed delivery and minimizing the company's carbon footprint.

Supporting Communities and Strengthening Local Impact
Community support remains central to Smithfield's sustainability strategy. Through its Helping Hungry Homes® hunger-relief program, the company delivered more than 16 million servings of protein valued at more than $18 million in 2025 to fight hunger and address food insecurity. Smithfield achieved its target in 2025 to donate 200 million servings of protein between 2020 and 2025, with 340 million servings donated since 2008.
The company also advanced employee-led community initiatives, with team members contributing thousands of volunteer hours and supporting local giving through Community Compass Committees across Smithfield's U.S. operations.
To learn more about Smithfield's sustainability programs, please visit .
About Smithfield Foods
Smithfield Foods, Inc. (Nasdaq: SFD) is an American food company with a leading position in packaged meats and fresh pork products. With a diverse brand portfolio and strong relationships with U.S. farmers and customers, we responsibly meet demand for quality protein around the world.

value.Title: WEEKLY CORPORATE NEWS TRACKER: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 2026

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-9DP3-RRPY-Y53M-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE NEWS TRACKER: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 20262026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:34ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE NEWS TRACKER: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 2026

+ YALE UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ YALE UNIVERSITY REVEALS UPDATED CLIMATE OPINION MAPS FOR 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects. The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support. Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions. The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

+ SOPHIE BUCHOWSKI JOINS YALE UNIVERSITY AS A PROMISING SCHOLAR ATHLETE

Sophie Buchowski, a standout athlete from John Burroughs School, has earned the title of the 2026 Post-Dispatch Scholar Athlete. This recognition highlights her exceptional performance both in academics and athletics, particularly in field hockey and lacrosse. Buchowski, who has a unique hobby of creating her own version of slime, will be taking her talents to Yale University, where she plans to continue her field hockey career while pursuing a degree in medicine. On the 5th of May, 2026, it was announced that Buchowski would join Yale University, a prestigious institution known for its rigorous academic programs and strong athletic programs. The Scholar Athlete initiative acknowledges students across various high schools who demonstrate excellence in sports as well as in the classroom. Buchowski's achievements in athletics and her commitment to academic excellence make her a fitting representative of this program. Buchowski's journey is not only marked by her athletic prowess but also by her academic aspirations. As she prepares to transition to Yale University, she aims to balance her demanding schedule of sports and studies. Her dedication to both her athletic and academic pursuits reflects a growing trend among student-athletes who aspire to excel in multiple domains. This achievement is a testament to her hard work and a source of inspiration for her peers. The Yale University athletic department has expressed excitement over Buchowski's commitment, emphasizing the value of student-athletes in fostering a culture of excellence. With a focus on developing athletes who are also scholars, Yale encourages its students to pursue their passions and goals, both on and off the field. Buchowski's dual commitment to sports and medicine aligns perfectly with this mission. As Buchowski embarks on this new chapter at Yale University, she joins a roster of talented student-athletes who have similarly balanced their academic and athletic careers. Her story serves as a reminder of the opportunities available to those who are dedicated and driven. The future looks bright for Sophie Buchowski as she steps onto the field at Yale, eager to make her mark both as an athlete and a future medical professional.

+ YALE UNIVERSITY UNVEILS NEW MISSION STATEMENT AMID TRUST CONCERNS

Yale University has introduced a new mission statement aimed at restoring public trust in higher education. The revised statement, which reads: 'Yale’s core mission is to create, disseminate, and preserve knowledge through research and teaching,' comes in the wake of a university-commissioned report released on 15th April 2026 that outlined key recommendations for enhancing the institution's credibility. The change marks a significant departure from Yale's previous mission statement, which was more expansive and included phrases such as 'improving the world today' and 'educating aspiring leaders worldwide.' Yale's new statement is notably concise, reflecting the report's emphasis on clarity and purpose in higher education. The committee of ten professors responsible for the report highlighted 'widespread uncertainty about the fundamental purpose and mission of higher education,' urging a more focused approach. Yale's President, Maurie McInnis, who initiated the committee's formation last year, acknowledged the report as an 'honest assessment' of the university's role in the decline of public trust. In her end-of-year message to the community, she indicated that the university had already begun implementing many of the committee's recommendations, including the adoption of this new mission statement. The report specifically criticized the previous mission statement's complexity, arguing that while its goals were commendable, they detracted from the core purpose of a university. The committee asserted that without a clear mission, it becomes challenging to measure whether educational institutions are fulfilling their commitments. The new statement aligns closely with a model referenced in the report, which aims to rebuild trust in Yale and similar institutions. In her message, McInnis emphasized the importance of public confidence in higher education, stating that the university's work must resonate with societal needs. She reaffirmed Yale's commitment to addressing global challenges and enhancing educational accessibility, including initiatives like free tuition for more students. As Yale continues to navigate the evolving landscape of higher education, McInnis plans to provide further updates in the coming months, reinforcing the institution's mission and priorities.

urn:contentItem:6JJH-00G3-RTSH-C3K2-00000-00WEEKLY DEVELOPMENTS REVIEW: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 20262026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:34ZLexisNexisWEEKLY DEVELOPMENTS REVIEW: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 2026

+ YALE UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ YALE UNIVERSITY REVEALS UPDATED CLIMATE OPINION MAPS FOR 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects. The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support. Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions. The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

+ SOPHIE BUCHOWSKI JOINS YALE UNIVERSITY AS A PROMISING SCHOLAR ATHLETE

Sophie Buchowski, a standout athlete from John Burroughs School, has earned the title of the 2026 Post-Dispatch Scholar Athlete. This recognition highlights her exceptional performance both in academics and athletics, particularly in field hockey and lacrosse. Buchowski, who has a unique hobby of creating her own version of slime, will be taking her talents to Yale University, where she plans to continue her field hockey career while pursuing a degree in medicine. On the 5th of May, 2026, it was announced that Buchowski would join Yale University, a prestigious institution known for its rigorous academic programs and strong athletic programs. The Scholar Athlete initiative acknowledges students across various high schools who demonstrate excellence in sports as well as in the classroom. Buchowski's achievements in athletics and her commitment to academic excellence make her a fitting representative of this program. Buchowski's journey is not only marked by her athletic prowess but also by her academic aspirations. As she prepares to transition to Yale University, she aims to balance her demanding schedule of sports and studies. Her dedication to both her athletic and academic pursuits reflects a growing trend among student-athletes who aspire to excel in multiple domains. This achievement is a testament to her hard work and a source of inspiration for her peers. The Yale University athletic department has expressed excitement over Buchowski's commitment, emphasizing the value of student-athletes in fostering a culture of excellence. With a focus on developing athletes who are also scholars, Yale encourages its students to pursue their passions and goals, both on and off the field. Buchowski's dual commitment to sports and medicine aligns perfectly with this mission. As Buchowski embarks on this new chapter at Yale University, she joins a roster of talented student-athletes who have similarly balanced their academic and athletic careers. Her story serves as a reminder of the opportunities available to those who are dedicated and driven. The future looks bright for Sophie Buchowski as she steps onto the field at Yale, eager to make her mark both as an athlete and a future medical professional.

+ YALE UNIVERSITY UNVEILS NEW MISSION STATEMENT AMID TRUST CONCERNS

Yale University has introduced a new mission statement aimed at restoring public trust in higher education. The revised statement, which reads: 'Yale’s core mission is to create, disseminate, and preserve knowledge through research and teaching,' comes in the wake of a university-commissioned report released on 15th April 2026 that outlined key recommendations for enhancing the institution's credibility. The change marks a significant departure from Yale's previous mission statement, which was more expansive and included phrases such as 'improving the world today' and 'educating aspiring leaders worldwide.' Yale's new statement is notably concise, reflecting the report's emphasis on clarity and purpose in higher education. The committee of ten professors responsible for the report highlighted 'widespread uncertainty about the fundamental purpose and mission of higher education,' urging a more focused approach. Yale's President, Maurie McInnis, who initiated the committee's formation last year, acknowledged the report as an 'honest assessment' of the university's role in the decline of public trust. In her end-of-year message to the community, she indicated that the university had already begun implementing many of the committee's recommendations, including the adoption of this new mission statement. The report specifically criticized the previous mission statement's complexity, arguing that while its goals were commendable, they detracted from the core purpose of a university. The committee asserted that without a clear mission, it becomes challenging to measure whether educational institutions are fulfilling their commitments. The new statement aligns closely with a model referenced in the report, which aims to rebuild trust in Yale and similar institutions. In her message, McInnis emphasized the importance of public confidence in higher education, stating that the university's work must resonate with societal needs. She reaffirmed Yale's commitment to addressing global challenges and enhancing educational accessibility, including initiatives like free tuition for more students. As Yale continues to navigate the evolving landscape of higher education, McInnis plans to provide further updates in the coming months, reinforcing the institution's mission and priorities.

urn:contentItem:6JJF-N343-RTCX-44PG-00000-00WEEKLY BUSINESS DEVELOPMENTS SNAPSHOT: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 20262026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:34ZLexisNexisWEEKLY BUSINESS DEVELOPMENTS SNAPSHOT: YALE UNIVERSITY NEWS THIS PAST WEEK MAY 10, 2026

+ YALE UNIVERSITY [3 News That Matters]

+ YALE UNIVERSITY REVEALS UPDATED CLIMATE OPINION MAPS FOR 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects. The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support. Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions. The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

+ SOPHIE BUCHOWSKI JOINS YALE UNIVERSITY AS A PROMISING SCHOLAR ATHLETE

Sophie Buchowski, a standout athlete from John Burroughs School, has earned the title of the 2026 Post-Dispatch Scholar Athlete. This recognition highlights her exceptional performance both in academics and athletics, particularly in field hockey and lacrosse. Buchowski, who has a unique hobby of creating her own version of slime, will be taking her talents to Yale University, where she plans to continue her field hockey career while pursuing a degree in medicine. On the 5th of May, 2026, it was announced that Buchowski would join Yale University, a prestigious institution known for its rigorous academic programs and strong athletic programs. The Scholar Athlete initiative acknowledges students across various high schools who demonstrate excellence in sports as well as in the classroom. Buchowski's achievements in athletics and her commitment to academic excellence make her a fitting representative of this program. Buchowski's journey is not only marked by her athletic prowess but also by her academic aspirations. As she prepares to transition to Yale University, she aims to balance her demanding schedule of sports and studies. Her dedication to both her athletic and academic pursuits reflects a growing trend among student-athletes who aspire to excel in multiple domains. This achievement is a testament to her hard work and a source of inspiration for her peers. The Yale University athletic department has expressed excitement over Buchowski's commitment, emphasizing the value of student-athletes in fostering a culture of excellence. With a focus on developing athletes who are also scholars, Yale encourages its students to pursue their passions and goals, both on and off the field. Buchowski's dual commitment to sports and medicine aligns perfectly with this mission. As Buchowski embarks on this new chapter at Yale University, she joins a roster of talented student-athletes who have similarly balanced their academic and athletic careers. Her story serves as a reminder of the opportunities available to those who are dedicated and driven. The future looks bright for Sophie Buchowski as she steps onto the field at Yale, eager to make her mark both as an athlete and a future medical professional.

+ YALE UNIVERSITY UNVEILS NEW MISSION STATEMENT AMID TRUST CONCERNS

Yale University has introduced a new mission statement aimed at restoring public trust in higher education. The revised statement, which reads: 'Yale’s core mission is to create, disseminate, and preserve knowledge through research and teaching,' comes in the wake of a university-commissioned report released on 15th April 2026 that outlined key recommendations for enhancing the institution's credibility. The change marks a significant departure from Yale's previous mission statement, which was more expansive and included phrases such as 'improving the world today' and 'educating aspiring leaders worldwide.' Yale's new statement is notably concise, reflecting the report's emphasis on clarity and purpose in higher education. The committee of ten professors responsible for the report highlighted 'widespread uncertainty about the fundamental purpose and mission of higher education,' urging a more focused approach. Yale's President, Maurie McInnis, who initiated the committee's formation last year, acknowledged the report as an 'honest assessment' of the university's role in the decline of public trust. In her end-of-year message to the community, she indicated that the university had already begun implementing many of the committee's recommendations, including the adoption of this new mission statement. The report specifically criticized the previous mission statement's complexity, arguing that while its goals were commendable, they detracted from the core purpose of a university. The committee asserted that without a clear mission, it becomes challenging to measure whether educational institutions are fulfilling their commitments. The new statement aligns closely with a model referenced in the report, which aims to rebuild trust in Yale and similar institutions. In her message, McInnis emphasized the importance of public confidence in higher education, stating that the university's work must resonate with societal needs. She reaffirmed Yale's commitment to addressing global challenges and enhancing educational accessibility, including initiatives like free tuition for more students. As Yale continues to navigate the evolving landscape of higher education, McInnis plans to provide further updates in the coming months, reinforcing the institution's mission and priorities.

value.Title: May 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ7-P0T3-S31K-S1WD-00000-00May 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS

Consigli Construction Co. is making significant strides with its recent involvement in the Williams College Museum of Art project, marking the museum's first purpose-built facility on campus. Set to begin on February 25, 2026, the project presents a unique design challenge that aims to enhance student access to art collections. Abigail Berkebile, a prominent figure at Consigli Construction Co., highlighted the project's complexity and the rewarding nature of collaborating with the museum staff. In conjunction with this development, Berkebile emphasized the vital role of women in the construction industry, noting that opportunities extend far beyond traditional field positions to include accounting, IT, legal, marketing, and design. By maintaining a structured approach to daily team collaboration, Consigli Construction Co. continues to focus on executing complex institutional builds with a diverse array of professional expertise.

In the broader sector, Suffolk Construction is advancing critical infrastructure and renewable energy initiatives. On May 5, 2026, the Virginia Department of Transportation is scheduled to lift a long-term lane closure on U.S. Route 58 westbound near downtown Suffolk, a change tied to the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by the firm. The project will ultimately provide a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to access a regional landfill. Concurrently, Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement on May 1, 2026, to source energy from a 70MW solar farm in Suffolk, which is currently under construction and expected to commence power generation in 2027. This solar initiative is projected to supply approximately five percent of the telecommunications company's total energy demands.

Turner Construction is also marking major structural milestones across multiple large-scale developments. On April 28, 2026, the company celebrated the topping out of the 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, a 450,000-square-foot facility featuring a hybrid cooling solution. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction, credited the milestone to strong collaboration with Aligned and the dedication of approximately 345 skilled workers logging over 80,000 craft hours. On the same day, Turner Construction announced the commencement of vertical construction for the $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania. The 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower will increase the hospital's capacity to 500 beds and is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028, supporting up to 3,800 construction jobs and generating an estimated $1 billion in local economic impact.

Gilbane Development continues to expand its leadership and strategic partnerships to drive future growth. On April 28, 2026, the firm appointed Andreas Vlahakis as the new head of capital markets, based in New York City. Reporting to President and CEO James Patchett, Vlahakis will direct the firm's funding strategy and lead equity-raising efforts for ground-up multifamily and student housing investments. Meanwhile, Gilbane is moving forward with a major public-private partnership at Western Kentucky University. Following approval by the university's Board of Regents in December 2025 and authorization from the Kentucky Legislature on April 6, 2026, Gilbane is now cleared to design and develop new residence halls to replace outdated campus housing, signaling a continuous push toward modernizing student facilities.

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 2025

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-DBX3-RXH7-2457-00000-00May 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 20252026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:34ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Yale University Reveals Updated Climate Opinion Maps for 2025

Yale University has released its updated Climate Opinion Maps, providing a detailed view of U.S. public sentiment regarding climate change and energy policy. This latest version, published on the 5th of May 2026, reveals a significant shift in attitudes toward renewable energy sources across the nation, particularly in support for solar and wind energy projects.

The updated Yale Climate Opinion Maps introduce four critical measures: support for local solar farms, local wind farms, opposition to fossil fuel drilling on public land, and preference for candidates who advocate for climate action. Notably, over half of the states in the U.S. show majority support for solar (44 states) and wind farms (29 states), indicating a clear trend towards renewable energy solutions in local communities.

Further analysis from Yale University indicates that a substantial 57% of Americans are inclined to vote for candidates who prioritize action on global warming. However, this preference varies significantly across different regions, from as low as 40% in West Virginia to as high as 87% in the District of Columbia. This local disparity highlights the complex relationship between geographic location and climate policy support.

Despite federal efforts to limit renewable energy projects, the momentum for solar and wind energy continues to grow, with notable increases in solar generation (up 37%) and wind energy (up 12%) from the previous year. States like California, Texas, and Florida lead the way in public support for local solar initiatives, reinforcing the trend of local advocacy for clean energy solutions.

The comprehensive data provided by the Yale Climate Opinion Maps serves as a vital resource for understanding public attitudes toward climate change and energy policies. As the country navigates a pivotal moment in addressing climate issues, Yale University's findings will be instrumental for policymakers, educators, and stakeholders aiming to align with the evolving public sentiment around renewable energy.

INDEX

SECTION 1 YALE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

SECTION 2 YALE UNIVERSITY TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 YALE UNIVERSITY PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Yale University, founded in 1701, is an Ivy League institution in New Haven, Connecticut. With around 15,000 employees and 12,000 students, it generates approximately $4.5 billion in annual revenue. Yale excels in humanities, sciences, and professional schools like law and medicine, with notable alumni including five U.S. presidents. In 2024, it expanded sustainability research. Competing with Harvard and Princeton, Yales mission is to educate leaders and advance discovery through rigorous scholarship and global outreach.

Mission

Yale's mission is to improve the world through exceptional research, education, and community engagement. By fostering a diverse, ethical, and collaborative environment, the university educates aspiring leaders dedicated to serving society across all sectors.

Vision

Yale aims to be the most student-focused research university globally. This vision is realized through faculty dedication to teaching, close engagement with students, and ensuring that all students benefit from Yale's extraordinary resources and programs.

Core Values

Excellence: Commitment to the highest standards in all endeavors.

Integrity: Ethical principles guide every decision and action.

Diversity and Inclusion: A welcoming environment that celebrates diverse perspectives and backgrounds.

Community: A collaborative culture that supports mutual growth and respect.

Service: Empowering members to positively impact society.

Research and Innovation

As a leader in research, Yale drives discovery across disciplines, preserving cultural and scientific heritage while inspiring innovation for future generations.

From its storied past to its ongoing contributions to society, Yale University continues to illuminate the world with its commitment to Lux et Veritas.

1.2 SUMMARY

value.Title: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - SECTOR UPDATES FROM PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-S813-S8K8-R2SN-00000-00SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - SECTOR UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:17:04ZLexisNexisSTOCKSUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - SECTOR UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

PermID5000317077Websitehttp://www.suffolk.com/IndustryConstruction & Engineering

ACTIVITIES:

Suffolk Construction, founded in 1982 by John Fish in Boston, Massachusetts, is a leading U.S. general contractor. With $5.5 billion in revenue and 2,500 employees, Suffolk delivers complex projects in healthcare, education, and commercial sectors. Notable works include Bostons Millennium Tower and Miamis Brickell City Centre. Employing advanced technologies like BIM and virtual design, Suffolk enhances project efficiency and safety. The company operates 10 offices nationwide, with a strong Northeast presence. Suffolks commitment to sustainability includes LEED-certified projects and eco-friendly practices. Its employee-centric culture, with robust training and diversity initiatives, earned it a spot on Fortunes Best Workplaces list. Suffolk continues to innovate, focusing on smart buildings and urban development.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again.The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. On 25th March 2026, the Commission unanimously passed the resolution, which supports the company’s expansion plans that include a new office tower, a parking deck expansion, a training and event center, and a wellness center.Commission President Jimmie Stephens emphasized the importance of Brasfield & Gorrie to the region, noting that the company has been a stalwart presence in Birmingham since its inception. "They’ve chosen to remain here although they are a worldwide company," he remarked, highlighting the firm's commitment to the local economy.The expansion represents a substantial investment of $64,584,500 from Brasfield & Gorrie, with $2,884,500 allocated for land acquisition, $56,100,000 designated for construction, and $5,600,000 earmarked for equipment. This development is expected to create 80 new jobs, each offering an hourly wage of $39.51. The county’s jobs grant, capped at $240,000, is designed to incentivize this growth.In return for its investment, Jefferson County anticipates a return of $8,000 to the general fund and $1,011,773 to the education fund, illustrating the potential benefits of supporting local businesses like Brasfield & Gorrie. Stephens expressed gratitude for the confidence the company has shown in the county, vowing to maintain a strong partnership moving forward.As the economic landscape continues to evolve, the expansion of Brasfield & Gorrie underscores the importance of local support for sustainable growth. The firm’s decision to invest in Birmingham not only strengthens its operational base but also contributes positively to the broader community.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE UNVEILS $64.5 MILLION EXPANSION PLANS FOR BIRMINGHAM HQ

Brasfield & Gorrie, one of Birmingham’s largest construction firms, is planning a major expansion at its national headquarters, a move company leaders say reflects continued growth and a long-term commitment to the city. The company presented plans to the Birmingham City Council committee on 10th March 2026 for a new office building and parking expansion at its headquarters campus along 7th Avenue South.The proposal outlines a project expected to bring millions of dollars in investment and dozens of new jobs to the city. Brasfield & Gorrie plans to construct a new four-story office building totaling roughly 80,000 square feet. According to company representatives, the first floor will serve as an event and training center designed to host gatherings and professional development opportunities, featuring a large banquet room, an auditorium, and several meeting and training rooms.The remaining three floors are planned for office space that will support Brasfield & Gorrie’s expanding national operations. Company leaders say the additional space will allow them to accommodate new employees while leaving room for future growth. In addition to the new building, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to add a fourth level to its existing parking deck, creating approximately 125 additional parking spaces for employees and visitors.As part of the expansion, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to create 80 new full-time jobs headquartered in Birmingham, with an expected average salary of about $82,000. During the council committee discussion, company leaders emphasized that the new roles will help support the firm’s operations across the country while keeping its headquarters firmly rooted in Birmingham. They stated, 'It will be jobs headquartered here in Birmingham to support our field and our operations. We’re really growing, and we’re committed to staying in Birmingham.'The project represents an estimated $64.5 million investment. Under the proposed agreement, the City of Birmingham would provide financial incentives of up to $80,000 to support the project. The proposal received approval from the council committee and will now move to the full Birmingham City Council for final consideration. If approved, company leaders say construction on the expansion could be completed by late 2027.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE RECEIVES $12M TAX INCENTIVE FOR JEFFERSON COUNTY PROJECT

Birmingham-based construction firm Brasfield & Gorrie has secured a substantial economic incentive package from state officials, aimed at bolstering its $65 million project in Jefferson County. On 20th February 2026, the Alabama Department of Commerce approved nearly $12 million in tax credits for the company, which includes $9.69 million in investment tax credits and $2.24 million in jobs tax credits.The incentives are part of a strategic initiative to promote economic development in the region and are expected to play a significant role in the execution of Brasfield & Gorrie's ambitious project. This funding will not only support the immediate development but also contribute to job creation and local investment, which aligns with the state's broader economic goals.Brasfield & Gorrie, a privately held firm recognized for its extensive portfolio in construction and project management, has been a key player in Alabama's construction landscape. The approval of these tax credits underscores the state's commitment to fostering growth within its local businesses, particularly in the construction sector, which has faced challenges amid fluctuating economic conditions.The $64.6 million project will likely enhance Brasfield & Gorrie's operational capabilities and expand its service offerings, further solidifying its market position. As the company moves forward with the development, stakeholders will be keenly observing how these incentives translate into tangible benefits for both Brasfield & Gorrie and the Jefferson County community.In conclusion, the Alabama Department of Commerce's support through this tax incentive package marks a significant milestone for Brasfield & Gorrie, reinforcing its role in driving economic progress in the region. The anticipated outcomes of this project will not only reflect on the company's growth trajectory but also on the overall economic vitality of Birmingham and its surrounding areas.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.
